- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- libnn-wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /libnn-optimizer learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- mlir-transform-writer: Write MLIR transform dialect scripts that directly control IR lowering - tiling, fusion, vectorization, bufferization, GPU thread mapping, shared memory promotion, and more. Use this skill whenever someone asks to generate, write, or modify a transform dialect script, transform library, codegen strategy, or tuning spec in MLIR. Also trigger when the request involves composing IR transformations for linalg ops, controlling IREE codegen pipelines via transform dialect, or replacing annotation-only configs with full transform strategies. Covers both upstream MLIR transform ops and IREE-specific extensions.
- spec-human: Create a human-reviewable architecture spec using a 6-section template (Summary, Background, Architecture, Interface, Edge Cases, Alternatives) BEFORE implementing a non-trivial design. Triggered explicitly via /spec-human <topic>, or when the user says "write a human spec", "create a review-ready design doc", "spec this out for review", or "I need a spec for X before we build it". Produces an ASCII markdown document at ./specs/<slug>.md aimed at peer engineers and tech leads who need to validate core architecture and trade-offs in 5 minutes. Do NOT auto-trigger on general design questions or coding tasks - this is a deliberate review gate that only fires on explicit request. For LLM-targeted specs (future sibling skill), use /spec-llm instead.
- iree-optimizer: Agentic per-dispatch tile-size tuner for IREE CPU codegen on RISC-V. Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. Trigger on "iree optimize", "optimize dispatch", "tile tune", or references to dispatch .mlir files for performance tuning.
- libnn-optimizer: Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
"libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
"phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
- cc-log: Lightweight system log to record all Claude Code behavior (tool use, subagent creation, compaction, permissions, session lifecycle) for analysis. Use when the user wants to enable/disable behavior logging, analyze Claude Code session logs, list saved logs, or understand Claude Code's tool usage patterns. Trigger on: "cc-log", "behavior log", "system log", "record behavior", "log enable", "log disable", "analyze log".
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# ROADMAP: `./hsim` end-user driver
2	
3	Tracks the rollout of a single, end-user-friendly entry point
4	(`./hsim`) that hides the developer-grade plumbing currently exposed
5	by `setup.sh`, `tests/e2e.sh`, and `scripts/run_e2e.sh`.
6	
7	Plan source-of-truth: `/home/nick/.claude/plans/i-need-to-design-purring-kahan.md`.
8	
9	One session = one feature. Update status when starting (in-progress)
10	and on completion (done). Each feature is its own commit (or a small
11	structural-then-behavioral pair), never mixed.
12	
13	## Status Legend
14	
15	- not-started: feature has not begun
16	- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
17	- done: RED test was written, GREEN landed, and the feature's done-criteria pass
18	
19	## Global Invariant
20	
21	After every feature lands, this must hold:
22	
23	```
24	JOBS=8 MODE=both bash scripts/run_e2e.sh
25	# pass list == 45 PASS / 0 FAIL  (baseline from e2e_ROADMAP.md F0)
26	```
27	
28	`./hsim` only WRAPS the existing scripts; it MUST NOT change their
29	behavior. Any divergence from the e2e baseline blocks the next feature.
30	
31	## Dependency DAG
32	
33	```
34	F1 (list) --> F2 (build) --> F3 (run) --> F4 (test) --> F5 (docs) --> F6 (verify)
35	                                                            |
36	                                                            +-- can also land any time after F4
37	```
38	
39	Strict serial order F1 -> F4 because each later feature reuses the
40	`Paths` / `run_subprocess` helpers introduced by the earlier ones.
41	F5 (docs) may land between F4 and F6 or after F6.
42	
43	## Pre-flight decisions (locked at plan time)
44	
45	1. **Entry point name** -- `./hsim` at repo root (executable Python script).
46	2. **Language** -- Python 3, stdlib only (no pip deps).
47	3. **Run scope** -- accepts both fixture-by-name and arbitrary ELF paths.
48	4. **Missing artifacts** -- fail with one-line hint, no auto-build.
49	5. **Trigger mode** -- explicit `--mode csr|pc` required in v1.
50	6. **Output style** -- quiet by default, `--verbose` flag exists.
51	7. **Out of scope for v1** -- `demo`, `doctor`, auto-detect mode, M3..M8.
52	
53	## Features
54	
55	### F1 - `./hsim list <fixtures|demos|modes>`
56	
57	- Status: done
58	- Type: behavioral (new code)
59	- Description: Lowest-risk subcommand -- pure parsing, no subprocess.
60	  Lets the user discover fixture names without grepping the Makefile.
61	- Key files:
62	  - **Create:** `hsim` (Python 3 script, ~80 lines for this feature only).
63	  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_list.sh` (RED test).
64	- Dependencies: none.
65	- Done criteria:
66	  - `./hsim list fixtures` exits 0 and outputs `rt_c_hello` and `rt_all_gprs`.
67	  - `./hsim list demos` exits 0 and outputs `demo_csr_oneshot` and `demo_pc_oneshot`.
68	  - `./hsim list modes` exits 0 and outputs `M1` and `M8`.
69	  - `./hsim list bogus` exits 2 with a usage hint.
70	  - The RED test in `tests/hsim/test_hsim_list.sh` is green.
71	
72	### F2 - `./hsim build [--what TARGET]`
73	
74	- Status: done
75	- Type: behavioral
76	- Description: One entry point for all artifact builds. Wraps
77	  `setup.sh` (qemu, vsim, plugin, simpoint) and
78	  `make -C tests/fixtures` (ELF fixtures). No reimplementation -- the
79	  subprocess delegates to the existing scripts.
80	- Key files:
81	  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `build` subparser).
82	  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_build_dry.sh` (RED test with `--dry-run`).
83	- Dependencies: F1 (shares argparse skeleton + `Paths` helper).
84	- Done criteria:
85	  - `./hsim build --dry-run` prints the underlying `bash setup.sh` /
86	    `make -C tests/fixtures` commands without executing.
87	  - `./hsim build --what qemu --dry-run` prints only the QEMU build line.
88	  - `./hsim build` from a clean checkout produces all four artifacts +
89	    fixtures and exits 0.
90	  - `setup.sh` is unchanged on disk.
91	
92	### F3 - `./hsim run <elf-or-fixture> --mode csr|pc`
93	
94	- Status: not-started
95	- Type: behavioral
96	- Description: One-shot round-trip runner. Wraps `tests/e2e.sh` with
97	  the right env vars. Resolves fixture-by-name (`rt_c_hello` ->
98	  `tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf`) AND arbitrary ELF paths
99	  (`my/app.elf`). Quiet 3-line progress output by default;
100	  `--verbose` dumps QEMU/vsim stdout inline.
101	- Key files:
102	  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `run` subparser + path-resolution helper +
103	    quiet/verbose output formatter).
104	  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_run_smoke.sh` (RED test).
105	- Dependencies: F1 (Paths helper), F2 (so artifacts can be built first).
106	- Done criteria:
107	  - `./hsim run rt_c_hello` (no `--mode`) exits 2 with usage hint.
108	  - `./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr` on a clean build exits 0 in <30s.
109	  - `./hsim run /abs/path/foo.elf --mode pc` resolves the path and runs.
110	  - `./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr` when vsim binary is absent
111	    exits 2 with `hsim: vsim not built -- run ./hsim build`.
112	  - `--verbose` prints the underlying e2e.sh stderr+stdout.
113	
114	### F4 - `./hsim test [--filter REGEX] [--mode csr|pc|both] [--jobs N]`
115	
116	- Status: not-started
117	- Type: behavioral
118	- Description: Wraps `scripts/run_e2e.sh` with the JOBS=8 cap pre-set
119	  (per project memory for the 128-core host). Parses the script's
120	  `results.tsv` and prints a summary line `PASS X, FAIL Y, SKIP Z`.
121	  On failure, prints the per-test log paths.
122	- Key files:
123	  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `test` subparser, results.tsv parser).
124	  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_test_small.sh` (RED test that
125	    runs `./hsim test --jobs 1 --filter rt_c_hello --mode csr`).
126	- Dependencies: F1, F2, F3.
127	- Done criteria:
128	  - `./hsim test --jobs 1 --filter rt_c_hello --mode csr` exits 0 and
129	    prints `PASS 1, FAIL 0, SKIP 0` (plus elapsed time).
130	  - `./hsim test` (no args) caps at JOBS=8 by default.
131	  - `./hsim test --jobs 4` overrides the cap.
132	  - `scripts/run_e2e.sh` is unchanged on disk.
133	
134	### F5 - Docs: README + USER_GUIDE + CLAUDE.md
135	
136	- Status: not-started
137	- Type: structural (no code change; doc edits)
138	- Description: Make `./hsim` the canonical entry point in every
139	  user-visible doc. The dev-facing env-var surface stays documented
140	  but is demoted to "escape hatch" sections.
141	- Key files:
142	  - **Modify:** `README.md` -- replace the stale `./build.sh`
143	    quickstart block (lines 12-22) with `./hsim` quickstart.
144	  - **Modify:** `docs/USER_GUIDE.md` -- prepend a new
145	    "Section 0.5: Quick start with `./hsim`" before the existing
146	    Section 1 build instructions. Keep Sections 1-7 unchanged.
147	  - **Modify:** `CLAUDE.md` -- under "Build Commands" add `./hsim build`
148	    and `./hsim test` as the primary path; mark raw scripts as
149	    developer escape hatch.
150	- Dependencies: F1..F4 (so the examples in docs work).
151	- Done criteria:
152	  - README quickstart is 3 lines and uses only `./hsim` commands.
153	  - USER_GUIDE Section 0.5 has a copy-pastable 4-command worked
154	    example.
155	  - CLAUDE.md still lists the underlying scripts for developers but
156	    points to `./hsim` first.
157	
158	### F6 - End-to-end verification
159	
160	- Status: not-started
161	- Type: no commit (safety net)
162	- Description: Run the 11-step smoke sequence from the plan against a
163	  clean checkout. Tick each step.
164	- Steps:
165	  ```
166	  ./hsim                                                  # help
167	  ./hsim list fixtures                                    # ~17 fixtures
168	  ./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr                        # exit 2, hint
169	  ./hsim build                                            # ~5 min cold
170	  ./hsim list fixtures                                    # unchanged
171	  ./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr                        # PASS ~6s
172	  ./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr -v                     # verbose
173	  ./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_all_gprs.elf --mode csr    # arbitrary path
174	  ./hsim run rt_c_v_regs --mode pc                        # PC trigger
175	  ./hsim test --filter rt_c --mode csr                    # ~7 tests
176	  ./hsim test                                             # full suite
177	  ```
178	- Dependencies: F1..F5.
179	- Done criteria: all 11 steps exit as expected; the full-suite line
180	  reports the same 45 PASS / 0 FAIL as the e2e baseline.
181	
182	## Out of scope (deferred to a future driver_ROADMAP_v2)
183	
184	- `./hsim demo <T1..T7>` -- wrap `tests/demos/demo_*.sh`.
185	- `./hsim doctor` -- env-health diagnoser.
186	- `./hsim run --mode auto` -- auto-detect via ELF inspection.
187	- Building user's own non-hybrid-compatible ELF (instrument it for them).
188	- M3..M8 (icount, BBV, slice, archive, replay) -- currently only
189	  reachable via the demo scripts.
190	- Bash-completion file.

# ROADMAP: `./hsim` end-user driver

Tracks the rollout of a single, end-user-friendly entry point
(`./hsim`) that hides the developer-grade plumbing currently exposed
by `setup.sh`, `tests/e2e.sh`, and `scripts/run_e2e.sh`.

Plan source-of-truth: `/home/nick/.claude/plans/i-need-to-design-purring-kahan.md`.

One session = one feature. Update status when starting (in-progress)
and on completion (done). Each feature is its own commit (or a small
structural-then-behavioral pair), never mixed.

## Status Legend

- not-started: feature has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: RED test was written, GREEN landed, and the feature's done-criteria pass

## Global Invariant

After every feature lands, this must hold:

```
JOBS=8 MODE=both bash scripts/run_e2e.sh
# pass list == 45 PASS / 0 FAIL  (baseline from e2e_ROADMAP.md F0)
```

`./hsim` only WRAPS the existing scripts; it MUST NOT change their
behavior. Any divergence from the e2e baseline blocks the next feature.

## Dependency DAG

```
F1 (list) --> F2 (build) --> F3 (run) --> F4 (test) --> F5 (docs) --> F6 (verify)
                                                            |
                                                            +-- can also land any time after F4
```

Strict serial order F1 -> F4 because each later feature reuses the
`Paths` / `run_subprocess` helpers introduced by the earlier ones.
F5 (docs) may land between F4 and F6 or after F6.

## Pre-flight decisions (locked at plan time)

1. **Entry point name** -- `./hsim` at repo root (executable Python script).
2. **Language** -- Python 3, stdlib only (no pip deps).
3. **Run scope** -- accepts both fixture-by-name and arbitrary ELF paths.
4. **Missing artifacts** -- fail with one-line hint, no auto-build.
5. **Trigger mode** -- explicit `--mode csr|pc` required in v1.
6. **Output style** -- quiet by default, `--verbose` flag exists.
7. **Out of scope for v1** -- `demo`, `doctor`, auto-detect mode, M3..M8.

## Features

### F1 - `./hsim list <fixtures|demos|modes>`

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral (new code)
- Description: Lowest-risk subcommand -- pure parsing, no subprocess.
  Lets the user discover fixture names without grepping the Makefile.
- Key files:
  - **Create:** `hsim` (Python 3 script, ~80 lines for this feature only).
  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_list.sh` (RED test).
- Dependencies: none.
- Done criteria:
  - `./hsim list fixtures` exits 0 and outputs `rt_c_hello` and `rt_all_gprs`.
  - `./hsim list demos` exits 0 and outputs `demo_csr_oneshot` and `demo_pc_oneshot`.
  - `./hsim list modes` exits 0 and outputs `M1` and `M8`.
  - `./hsim list bogus` exits 2 with a usage hint.
  - The RED test in `tests/hsim/test_hsim_list.sh` is green.

### F2 - `./hsim build [--what TARGET]`

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: One entry point for all artifact builds. Wraps
  `setup.sh` (qemu, vsim, plugin, simpoint) and
  `make -C tests/fixtures` (ELF fixtures). No reimplementation -- the
  subprocess delegates to the existing scripts.
- Key files:
  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `build` subparser).
  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_build_dry.sh` (RED test with `--dry-run`).
- Dependencies: F1 (shares argparse skeleton + `Paths` helper).
- Done criteria:
  - `./hsim build --dry-run` prints the underlying `bash setup.sh` /
    `make -C tests/fixtures` commands without executing.
  - `./hsim build --what qemu --dry-run` prints only the QEMU build line.
  - `./hsim build` from a clean checkout produces all four artifacts +
    fixtures and exits 0.
  - `setup.sh` is unchanged on disk.

### F3 - `./hsim run <elf-or-fixture> --mode csr|pc`

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: One-shot round-trip runner. Wraps `tests/e2e.sh` with
  the right env vars. Resolves fixture-by-name (`rt_c_hello` ->
  `tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf`) AND arbitrary ELF paths
  (`my/app.elf`). Quiet 3-line progress output by default;
  `--verbose` dumps QEMU/vsim stdout inline.
- Key files:
  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `run` subparser + path-resolution helper +
    quiet/verbose output formatter).
  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_run_smoke.sh` (RED test).
- Dependencies: F1 (Paths helper), F2 (so artifacts can be built first).
- Done criteria:
  - `./hsim run rt_c_hello` (no `--mode`) exits 2 with usage hint.
  - `./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr` on a clean build exits 0 in <30s.
  - `./hsim run /abs/path/foo.elf --mode pc` resolves the path and runs.
  - `./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr` when vsim binary is absent
    exits 2 with `hsim: vsim not built -- run ./hsim build`.
  - `--verbose` prints the underlying e2e.sh stderr+stdout.

### F4 - `./hsim test [--filter REGEX] [--mode csr|pc|both] [--jobs N]`

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Wraps `scripts/run_e2e.sh` with the JOBS=8 cap pre-set
  (per project memory for the 128-core host). Parses the script's
  `results.tsv` and prints a summary line `PASS X, FAIL Y, SKIP Z`.
  On failure, prints the per-test log paths.
- Key files:
  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `test` subparser, results.tsv parser).
  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_test_small.sh` (RED test that
    runs `./hsim test --jobs 1 --filter rt_c_hello --mode csr`).
- Dependencies: F1, F2, F3.
- Done criteria:
  - `./hsim test --jobs 1 --filter rt_c_hello --mode csr` exits 0 and
    prints `PASS 1, FAIL 0, SKIP 0` (plus elapsed time).
  - `./hsim test` (no args) caps at JOBS=8 by default.
  - `./hsim test --jobs 4` overrides the cap.
  - `scripts/run_e2e.sh` is unchanged on disk.

### F5 - Docs: README + USER_GUIDE + CLAUDE.md

- Status: not-started
- Type: structural (no code change; doc edits)
- Description: Make `./hsim` the canonical entry point in every
  user-visible doc. The dev-facing env-var surface stays documented
  but is demoted to "escape hatch" sections.
- Key files:
  - **Modify:** `README.md` -- replace the stale `./build.sh`
    quickstart block (lines 12-22) with `./hsim` quickstart.
  - **Modify:** `docs/USER_GUIDE.md` -- prepend a new
    "Section 0.5: Quick start with `./hsim`" before the existing
    Section 1 build instructions. Keep Sections 1-7 unchanged.
  - **Modify:** `CLAUDE.md` -- under "Build Commands" add `./hsim build`
    and `./hsim test` as the primary path; mark raw scripts as
    developer escape hatch.
- Dependencies: F1..F4 (so the examples in docs work).
- Done criteria:
  - README quickstart is 3 lines and uses only `./hsim` commands.
  - USER_GUIDE Section 0.5 has a copy-pastable 4-command worked
    example.
  - CLAUDE.md still lists the underlying scripts for developers but
    points to `./hsim` first.

### F6 - End-to-end verification

- Status: not-started
- Type: no commit (safety net)
- Description: Run the 11-step smoke sequence from the plan against a
  clean checkout. Tick each step.
- Steps:
  ```
  ./hsim                                                  # help
  ./hsim list fixtures                                    # ~17 fixtures
  ./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr                        # exit 2, hint
  ./hsim build                                            # ~5 min cold
  ./hsim list fixtures                                    # unchanged
  ./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr                        # PASS ~6s
  ./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr -v                     # verbose
  ./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_all_gprs.elf --mode csr    # arbitrary path
  ./hsim run rt_c_v_regs --mode pc                        # PC trigger
  ./hsim test --filter rt_c --mode csr                    # ~7 tests
  ./hsim test                                             # full suite
  ```
- Dependencies: F1..F5.
- Done criteria: all 11 steps exit as expected; the full-suite line
  reports the same 45 PASS / 0 FAIL as the e2e baseline.

## Out of scope (deferred to a future driver_ROADMAP_v2)

- `./hsim demo <T1..T7>` -- wrap `tests/demos/demo_*.sh`.
- `./hsim doctor` -- env-health diagnoser.
- `./hsim run --mode auto` -- auto-detect via ELF inspection.
- Building user's own non-hybrid-compatible ELF (instrument it for them).
- M3..M8 (icount, BBV, slice, archive, replay) -- currently only
  reachable via the demo scripts.
- Bash-completion file.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""hsim - end-user entry point for the Andes hybrid simulator.
3	
4	Single-file driver. Wraps the developer-grade scripts (setup.sh,
5	tests/e2e.sh, scripts/run_e2e.sh) behind four subcommands:
6	
7	    ./hsim build [--what TARGET]
8	    ./hsim run <elf-or-fixture> --mode csr|pc
9	    ./hsim test [--filter REGEX] [--mode csr|pc|both]
10	    ./hsim list <fixtures|demos|modes>
11	
12	Python 3 stdlib only; no pip deps.
13	"""
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import argparse
17	import re
18	import shlex

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""hsim - end-user entry point for the Andes hybrid simulator.

Single-file driver. Wraps the developer-grade scripts (setup.sh,
tests/e2e.sh, scripts/run_e2e.sh) behind four subcommands:

    ./hsim build [--what TARGET]
    ./hsim run <elf-or-fixture> --mode csr|pc
    ./hsim test [--filter REGEX] [--mode csr|pc|both]
    ./hsim list <fixtures|demos|modes>

Python 3 stdlib only; no pip deps.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import re
import shlex
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent


# --- F1: list ---------------------------------------------------------------

# M1..M8 short table. Authoritative source: docs/USER_GUIDE.md sections 3-4.
# Kept here so `./hsim list modes` does not need to grep USER_GUIDE at runtime.
MODES = [
    ("M1", "csr",           "csrwi 0x7C0, {0,1} insn match (default)"),
    ("M2", "pc",            "ELF symbols _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc"),
    ("M3", "icount",        "retired-insn count trigger"),
    ("M4", "bbv",           "basic-block vector profiling"),
    ("M5", "slice",         "slice-consume; vsim resumes from a captured slice"),
    ("M6", "archive",       "checkpoint archive replay"),
    ("M7", "replay",        "deterministic re-execution of a captured run"),
    ("M8", "end-of-program", "natural program exit terminates the round-trip"),
]


def parse_fixtures(makefile: Path) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
    """Extract fixture names + family from tests/fixtures/Makefile.

    Returns rows (name, family) in stable, family-grouped order. Family is
    the variable they came from (RT_FIXTURES, RT_C_FIXTURES, ...).
    """
    text = makefile.read_text()
    rows: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
    # Variable definitions look like:
    #   RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases \
    #                  rt_long_roi
    # We capture everything up to a non-continuation newline.
    var_pattern = re.compile(
        r"^(?P<var>[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*)\s*:?=\s*(?P<body>(?:[^\n]*\\\n)*[^\n]*)$",
        re.MULTILINE,
    )
    interesting = {
        "RT_FIXTURES":      "asm",
        "RT_C_FIXTURES":    "c",
        "RT_C_V_FIXTURES":  "c+rvv",
        "PROBE_V_FIXTURES": "probe",
    }
    seen: set[str] = set()
    for m in var_pattern.finditer(text):
        var = m.group("var")
        if var not in interesting:
            continue
        family = interesting[var]
        body = m.group("body").replace("\\\n", " ")
        for name in body.split():
            if name and name not in seen:
                rows.append((name, family))
                seen.add(name)
    # Pick up the legacy hand-listed ELF targets.
    for name in ("handoff_roundtrip",):
        if name not in seen:
            rows.append((name, "asm"))
            seen.add(name)
    return rows


def list_fixtures() -> int:
    makefile = REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "fixtures" / "Makefile"
    if not makefile.is_file():
        print(f"hsim: missing {makefile}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    rows = parse_fixtures(makefile)
    width = max(len(name) for name, _ in rows)
    print(f"{'FIXTURE'.ljust(width)}  FAMILY")
    for name, family in rows:
        print(f"{name.ljust(width)}  {family}")
    return 0


def list_demos() -> int:
    demo_dir = REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "demos"
    if not demo_dir.is_dir():
        print(f"hsim: missing {demo_dir}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    names = sorted(p.stem for p in demo_dir.glob("demo_*.sh"))
    if not names:
        print(f"hsim: no demos found under {demo_dir}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    for name in names:
        print(name)
    return 0


def list_modes() -> int:
    width_id = max(len(m[0]) for m in MODES)
    width_key = max(len(m[1]) for m in MODES)
    print(f"{'ID'.ljust(width_id)}  {'KEY'.ljust(width_key)}  DESCRIPTION")
    for mode_id, key, desc in MODES:
        print(f"{mode_id.ljust(width_id)}  {key.ljust(width_key)}  {desc}")
    return 0


def cmd_list(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    return {
        "fixtures": list_fixtures,
        "demos":    list_demos,
        "modes":    list_modes,
    }[args.target]()


# --- F2: build --------------------------------------------------------------

# Each target maps to a list of subprocess argv lists. We delegate to the
# existing scripts (setup.sh, scripts/build_*.sh, Makefiles) -- no logic
# duplicated here.
def _build_steps(target: str) -> list[list[str]]:
    qemu     = ["bash", "scripts/build_qemu.sh"]
    vsim     = ["bash", "scripts/build_vsim.sh"]
    plugin   = ["make", "-C", "qemu_plugin", "QEMU_ROOT=./QEMU"]
    simpoint = ["bash", "scripts/build_simpoint.sh"]
    fixtures = ["make", "-C", "tests/fixtures"]
    setup    = ["bash", "setup.sh"]
    return {
        "all":      [setup, fixtures],
        "qemu":     [qemu],
        "vsim":     [vsim],
        "plugin":   [plugin],
        "simpoint": [simpoint],
        "fixtures": [fixtures],
    }[target]


def cmd_build(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    try:
        steps = _build_steps(args.what)
    except KeyError:
        print(f"hsim: unknown build target: {args.what}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    for argv in steps:
        line = shlex.join(argv)
        if args.dry_run:
            print(line)
            continue
        print(f"[hsim] {line}")
        rc = subprocess.run(argv, cwd=REPO_ROOT).returncode
        if rc != 0:
            print(f"hsim: build step failed (rc={rc}): {line}", file=sys.stderr)
            return rc
    return 0


# --- main -------------------------------------------------------------------

def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="hsim",
        description="End-user driver for the Andes hybrid simulator (QEMU + vsim).",
    )
    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="cmd", metavar="<cmd>")
    sub.required = True

    p_list = sub.add_parser(
        "list",
        help="list available fixtures, demos, or trigger modes",
    )
    p_list.add_argument(
        "target",
        choices=("fixtures", "demos", "modes"),
        help="what to list",
    )
    p_list.set_defaults(func=cmd_list)

    p_build = sub.add_parser(
        "build",
        help="build artifacts (qemu, vsim, plugin, simpoint, ELF fixtures)",
    )
    p_build.add_argument(
        "--what",
        choices=("all", "qemu", "vsim", "plugin", "simpoint", "fixtures"),
        default="all",
        help="which target to build (default: all)",
    )
    p_build.add_argument(
        "--dry-run",
        action="store_true",
        help="print the underlying commands without executing",
    )
    p_build.set_defaults(func=cmd_build)

    return parser


def main(argv: list[str]) -> int:
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
    return args.func(args)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main(sys.argv[1:]))

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# End-to-end roundtrip harness: QEMU 1 + plugin -> vsim -> QEMU 2.
3	#
4	# Memory between QEMU and vsim is shared via an /dev/shm-backed mmap
5	# (128 MB). Works for both asm fixtures (handoff_roundtrip, rt_*) and
6	# C-level fixtures (rt_c_*). C fixtures require shared mmap so the
7	# stack/heap survives handoffs; asm fixtures don't need it but run
8	# fine with it.
9	#
10	# Env:
11	#   FIXTURE         REQUIRED. .elf name under tests/fixtures/ (no .elf suffix).
12	#   EXPECT_RC       Final exit code to assert (default 0).
13	#   TRIGGER_MODE    csr | pc (default csr).
14	#   TIMEOUT         vsim leg timeout in seconds (default 60).
15	#   CHECK_MCYCLE_MONOTONIC=1   assert mcycle/minstret strictly increased
16	#                              between QEMU 1's drain and vsim's drain.
17	#   VSIM_BIN, VSIM_QEMU, VSIM_PLUGIN, VSIM_ELF, NM  override built paths.
18	
19	set -euo pipefail
20	
21	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
22	. "$SCRIPT_DIR/lib/paths.sh"
23	
24	FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:?FIXTURE env var is required (e.g. FIXTURE=rt_c_hello)}"
25	EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
26	TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
27	TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"
28	
29	hybrid_resolve_paths
30	
31	NM="${NM:-${HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN:+$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}}"
32	NM="${NM:-riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"
33	
34	case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
35	  csr) ;;
36	  pc)
37	    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
38	      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
39	      exit 77
40	    fi
41	    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
42	    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
43	    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT"  | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
44	    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
45	      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
46	      exit 1
47	    fi
48	    ;;
49	  *)
50	    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
51	    exit 1
52	    ;;
53	esac
54	
55	# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim uses the same size.
56	SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
57	SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-e2e-shared.XXXXXX)
58	STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
59	STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
60	SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
61	trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT
62	truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"
63	
64	PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
65	VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
66	if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
67	  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
68	  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
69	fi
70	
71	echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1: drain entry state"
72	set +e
73	"$VSIM_QEMU" \
74	  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
75	  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
76	  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
77	  -nographic \
78	  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
79	  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
80	  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1
81	QEMU1_RC=$?
82	set -e
83	
84	if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
85	  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
86	  exit 1
87	fi
88	
89	if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
90	  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
91	  exit 1
92	fi
93	
94	echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
95	set +e
96	timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
97	  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
98	  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
99	  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
100	  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
101	  --shared-mem-base 0x00000000 \
102	  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
103	  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
104	RC=$?
105	set -e
106	
107	# Opt-in: mcycle/minstret must strictly increase from QEMU 1's drain
108	# to vsim's drain. Offsets locked by state_abi_check.h (mcycle@624,
109	# minstret@632). Runs before the RC gate so the verdict is independent
110	# of any downstream-phase failure.
111	if [ "${CHECK_MCYCLE_MONOTONIC:-0}" = "1" ] && [ -s "$STATE_VSIM" ]; then
112	  read_u64_le() { od -A n -j "$1" -N 8 -t u8 "$2" | tr -d ' \n'; }
113	  Q1_MCYCLE=$(read_u64_le 624 "$STATE_QEMU")
114	  Q1_MINST=$(read_u64_le  632 "$STATE_QEMU")
115	  V_MCYCLE=$(read_u64_le  624 "$STATE_VSIM")
116	  V_MINST=$(read_u64_le   632 "$STATE_VSIM")
117	  echo "[$FIXTURE mcycle/minstret] qemu1: mcycle=$Q1_MCYCLE minstret=$Q1_MINST"
118	  echo "[$FIXTURE mcycle/minstret] vsim:  mcycle=$V_MCYCLE minstret=$V_MINST"
119	  if [ "$V_MCYCLE" -le "$Q1_MCYCLE" ]; then
120	    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: mcycle not strictly increasing across vsim"
121	    exit 1
122	  fi
123	  if [ "$V_MINST" -le "$Q1_MINST" ]; then
124	    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: minstret not strictly increasing across vsim"
125	    exit 1
126	  fi
127	  echo "[$FIXTURE mcycle/minstret] PASS: strictly increasing"
128	fi
129	
130	if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
131	  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
132	  echo "--- sim log ---"
133	  cat "$SIM_LOG"
134	  exit 1
135	fi
136	
137	echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"
138

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# End-to-end roundtrip harness: QEMU 1 + plugin -> vsim -> QEMU 2.
#
# Memory between QEMU and vsim is shared via an /dev/shm-backed mmap
# (128 MB). Works for both asm fixtures (handoff_roundtrip, rt_*) and
# C-level fixtures (rt_c_*). C fixtures require shared mmap so the
# stack/heap survives handoffs; asm fixtures don't need it but run
# fine with it.
#
# Env:
#   FIXTURE         REQUIRED. .elf name under tests/fixtures/ (no .elf suffix).
#   EXPECT_RC       Final exit code to assert (default 0).
#   TRIGGER_MODE    csr | pc (default csr).
#   TIMEOUT         vsim leg timeout in seconds (default 60).
#   CHECK_MCYCLE_MONOTONIC=1   assert mcycle/minstret strictly increased
#                              between QEMU 1's drain and vsim's drain.
#   VSIM_BIN, VSIM_QEMU, VSIM_PLUGIN, VSIM_ELF, NM  override built paths.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
. "$SCRIPT_DIR/lib/paths.sh"

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:?FIXTURE env var is required (e.g. FIXTURE=rt_c_hello)}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

hybrid_resolve_paths

NM="${NM:-${HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN:+$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}}"
NM="${NM:-riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT"  | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim uses the same size.
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-e2e-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT
truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1: drain entry state"
set +e
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1
QEMU1_RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
  --shared-mem-base 0x00000000 \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

# Opt-in: mcycle/minstret must strictly increase from QEMU 1's drain
# to vsim's drain. Offsets locked by state_abi_check.h (mcycle@624,
# minstret@632). Runs before the RC gate so the verdict is independent
# of any downstream-phase failure.
if [ "${CHECK_MCYCLE_MONOTONIC:-0}" = "1" ] && [ -s "$STATE_VSIM" ]; then
  read_u64_le() { od -A n -j "$1" -N 8 -t u8 "$2" | tr -d ' \n'; }
  Q1_MCYCLE=$(read_u64_le 624 "$STATE_QEMU")
  Q1_MINST=$(read_u64_le  632 "$STATE_QEMU")
  V_MCYCLE=$(read_u64_le  624 "$STATE_VSIM")
  V_MINST=$(read_u64_le   632 "$STATE_VSIM")
  echo "[$FIXTURE mcycle/minstret] qemu1: mcycle=$Q1_MCYCLE minstret=$Q1_MINST"
  echo "[$FIXTURE mcycle/minstret] vsim:  mcycle=$V_MCYCLE minstret=$V_MINST"
  if [ "$V_MCYCLE" -le "$Q1_MCYCLE" ]; then
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: mcycle not strictly increasing across vsim"
    exit 1
  fi
  if [ "$V_MINST" -le "$Q1_MINST" ]; then
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: minstret not strictly increasing across vsim"
    exit 1
  fi
  echo "[$FIXTURE mcycle/minstret] PASS: strictly increasing"
fi

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# RED test for F1: `./hsim list <fixtures|demos|modes>`.
#
# Asserts the discoverability surface of the new driver. Does not need
# any artifacts built; pure parsing.

set -uo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
HSIM="$ROOT/hsim"

fail() { echo "[FAIL] $*" >&2; exit 1; }
ok()   { echo "[ok] $*"; }

[ -x "$HSIM" ] || fail "missing executable: $HSIM"

# fixtures: must include at least one asm fixture and one C fixture.
out=$("$HSIM" list fixtures 2>&1) || fail "list fixtures exit nonzero: $out"
echo "$out" | grep -qw rt_all_gprs || fail "list fixtures missing rt_all_gprs:\n$out"
echo "$out" | grep -qw rt_c_hello  || fail "list fixtures missing rt_c_hello:\n$out"
ok "list fixtures includes rt_all_gprs and rt_c_hello"

# demos: must include the M1 (csr) and M2 (pc) demos.
out=$("$HSIM" list demos 2>&1) || fail "list demos exit nonzero: $out"
echo "$out" | grep -qw demo_csr_oneshot || fail "list demos missing demo_csr_oneshot:\n$out"
echo "$out" | grep -qw demo_pc_oneshot  || fail "list demos missing demo_pc_oneshot:\n$out"
ok "list demos includes demo_csr_oneshot and demo_pc_oneshot"

# modes: must include M1 and M8 from USER_GUIDE.
out=$("$HSIM" list modes 2>&1) || fail "list modes exit nonzero: $out"
echo "$out" | grep -qw M1 || fail "list modes missing M1:\n$out"
echo "$out" | grep -qw M8 || fail "list modes missing M8:\n$out"
ok "list modes includes M1 and M8"

# bogus arg: must exit non-zero with a usage hint.
set +e
out=$("$HSIM" list bogus 2>&1)
rc=$?
set -e
[ "$rc" -ne 0 ] || fail "list bogus should exit non-zero, got 0; output: $out"
ok "list bogus exits non-zero (rc=$rc)"

echo "PASS test_hsim_list"

2	# RED test for F2: `./hsim build [--what TARGET] --dry-run`.
3	#
4	# --dry-run must print the underlying commands without executing them.
5	# This lets us unit-test the build subparser quickly (the real build
6	# takes ~5 min and is exercised by F6 verification).
7	
8	set -uo pipefail
9	
10	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
11	ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
12	HSIM="$ROOT/hsim"
13	
14	fail() { echo "[FAIL] $*" >&2; exit 1; }
15	ok()   { echo "[ok] $*"; }
16	
17	[ -x "$HSIM" ] || fail "missing executable: $HSIM"
18	
19	# 1. Default (no --what): dry-run lists ALL the underlying steps:
20	#    bash setup.sh + make -C tests/fixtures.
21	out=$("$HSIM" build --dry-run 2>&1) || fail "build --dry-run exit nonzero: $out"
22	echo "$out" | grep -q "bash .*setup\.sh"          || fail "missing setup.sh line:\n$out"
23	echo "$out" | grep -q "make .*-C .*tests/fixtures" || fail "missing tests/fixtures line:\n$out"
24	ok "build --dry-run lists setup.sh and tests/fixtures"
25	
26	# 2. --what qemu: dry-run lists ONLY the qemu builder, not vsim/plugin/simpoint/fixtures.
27	out=$("$HSIM" build --what qemu --dry-run 2>&1) || fail "build --what qemu --dry-run exit nonzero: $out"
28	echo "$out" | grep -q "build_qemu\.sh"           || fail "missing build_qemu.sh:\n$out"
29	echo "$out" | grep -q "build_vsim\.sh"           && fail "should NOT list vsim:\n$out"
30	echo "$out" | grep -q "build_simpoint\.sh"       && fail "should NOT list simpoint:\n$out"
31	echo "$out" | grep -q "tests/fixtures"           && fail "should NOT list fixtures:\n$out"
32	ok "build --what qemu --dry-run lists only qemu"
33	
34	# 3. --what fixtures: only the make line.
35	out=$("$HSIM" build --what fixtures --dry-run 2>&1) || fail "build --what fixtures --dry-run exit nonzero: $out"
36	echo "$out" | grep -q "make .*-C .*tests/fixtures" || fail "missing tests/fixtures line:\n$out"
37	echo "$out" | grep -q "setup\.sh"                  && fail "should NOT list setup.sh:\n$out"
38	ok "build --what fixtures --dry-run lists only fixtures"
39	
40	# 4. --what bogus: exit non-zero with usage hint.
41	set +e
42	out=$("$HSIM" build --what bogus --dry-run 2>&1)
43	rc=$?
44	set -e
45	[ "$rc" -ne 0 ] || fail "build --what bogus should exit non-zero, got 0; output: $out"
46	ok "build --what bogus exits non-zero (rc=$rc)"
47	
48	# 5. Verify setup.sh is NOT modified by a dry-run.
49	#    We compare against git's index for the file.
50	diff -q <(git -C "$ROOT" show HEAD:setup.sh) "$ROOT/setup.sh" >/dev/null \
51	  || fail "setup.sh differs from HEAD (must be unchanged)"
52	ok "setup.sh unchanged from HEAD"
53	
54	echo "PASS test_hsim_build_dry"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# RED test for F2: `./hsim build [--what TARGET] --dry-run`.
#
# --dry-run must print the underlying commands without executing them.
# This lets us unit-test the build subparser quickly (the real build
# takes ~5 min and is exercised by F6 verification).

set -uo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
HSIM="$ROOT/hsim"

fail() { echo "[FAIL] $*" >&2; exit 1; }
ok()   { echo "[ok] $*"; }

[ -x "$HSIM" ] || fail "missing executable: $HSIM"

# 1. Default (no --what): dry-run lists ALL the underlying steps:
#    bash setup.sh + make -C tests/fixtures.
out=$("$HSIM" build --dry-run 2>&1) || fail "build --dry-run exit nonzero: $out"
echo "$out" | grep -q "bash .*setup\.sh"          || fail "missing setup.sh line:\n$out"
echo "$out" | grep -q "make .*-C .*tests/fixtures" || fail "missing tests/fixtures line:\n$out"
ok "build --dry-run lists setup.sh and tests/fixtures"

# 2. --what qemu: dry-run lists ONLY the qemu builder, not vsim/plugin/simpoint/fixtures.
out=$("$HSIM" build --what qemu --dry-run 2>&1) || fail "build --what qemu --dry-run exit nonzero: $out"
echo "$out" | grep -q "build_qemu\.sh"           || fail "missing build_qemu.sh:\n$out"
echo "$out" | grep -q "build_vsim\.sh"           && fail "should NOT list vsim:\n$out"
echo "$out" | grep -q "build_simpoint\.sh"       && fail "should NOT list simpoint:\n$out"
echo "$out" | grep -q "tests/fixtures"           && fail "should NOT list fixtures:\n$out"
ok "build --what qemu --dry-run lists only qemu"

# 3. --what fixtures: only the make line.
out=$("$HSIM" build --what fixtures --dry-run 2>&1) || fail "build --what fixtures --dry-run exit nonzero: $out"
echo "$out" | grep -q "make .*-C .*tests/fixtures" || fail "missing tests/fixtures line:\n$out"
echo "$out" | grep -q "setup\.sh"                  && fail "should NOT list setup.sh:\n$out"
ok "build --what fixtures --dry-run lists only fixtures"

# 4. --what bogus: exit non-zero with usage hint.
set +e
out=$("$HSIM" build --what bogus --dry-run 2>&1)
rc=$?
set -e
[ "$rc" -ne 0 ] || fail "build --what bogus should exit non-zero, got 0; output: $out"
ok "build --what bogus exits non-zero (rc=$rc)"

# 5. Verify setup.sh is NOT modified by a dry-run.
#    We compare against git's index for the file.
diff -q <(git -C "$ROOT" show HEAD:setup.sh) "$ROOT/setup.sh" >/dev/null \
  || fail "setup.sh differs from HEAD (must be unchanged)"
ok "setup.sh unchanged from HEAD"

echo "PASS test_hsim_build_dry"

file.content:
# Shared path resolution for the top-level e2e harness + drivers.
# Sourced (not executed). Provides:
#   TOP_ROOT                  - absolute path of the repo root
#   hybrid_resolve_paths()    - sets VSIM_BIN, VSIM_QEMU, VSIM_PLUGIN,
#                               VSIM_ELF from TOP_ROOT (unless already
#                               exported by caller) and exits 77 (CTest
#                               skip) if any artifact is missing. Reads
#                               FIXTURE for the VSIM_ELF default.

TESTS_LIB_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
TOP_ROOT=$(cd -- "$TESTS_LIB_DIR/../.." && pwd)

if [ -z "${HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN:-}" ] && [ -f "$TOP_ROOT/config.env" ]; then
  . "$TOP_ROOT/config.env"
fi

hybrid_resolve_paths() {
  VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$TOP_ROOT/verilator/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
  VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$TOP_ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
  VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$TOP_ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
  VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$TOP_ROOT/tests/fixtures/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
  for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
    if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
      echo "skip: $f not built; run 'source ./setup.sh' and 'make -C tests/fixtures'"
      exit 77
    fi
  done
}

F3 plan and clarifying questions before I write code.

**Approach.** RED: `tests/hsim/test_hsim_run_smoke.sh` covers (a) `run rt_c_hello` without `--mode` -> rc=2 with hint, (b) bogus fixture name -> rc=2 with hint, (c) `--verbose` flag is accepted (`--help` lists it), and (d) one happy-path `run rt_c_hello --mode csr` exits 0 in <30s.  Then GREEN: add `run` subparser in `hsim` that resolves `<name>` -> `tests/fixtures/<name>.elf` (else treats arg as a path), pre-flights `VSIM_BIN`/`VSIM_QEMU`/`VSIM_PLUGIN`/`<elf>` and prints `hsim: vsim not built -- run ./hsim build` style hints on the missing one, then `subprocess.run(["bash","tests/e2e.sh"], env={FIXTURE,TRIGGER_MODE,VSIM_ELF})`. Quiet by default streams only the e2e.sh `[fixture N/3 ...]` lines (3 lines); `--verbose` inherits stdout/stderr.

Two small clarifications before I code:

questions.question: For `./hsim run`, how should the positional ELF/fixture arg be resolved when the user passes a bare name (e.g. `rt_c_hello`)?

options.label: Name -> tests/fixtures/<name>.elf only (Recommended)
options.description: If arg looks like a bare name (no '/' and no '.elf'), join to tests/fixtures/<name>.elf. If arg contains '/' or ends in '.elf', treat as a path (relative-to-CWD or absolute). This matches the ROADMAP wording 'fixture-by-name AND arbitrary ELF paths'.

options.label: Always try fixtures/ first, fall back to path
options.description: Try tests/fixtures/<arg>.elf first; if that doesn't exist, treat arg as a path. Slightly more magic; could mask typos when a same-named file exists under cwd.

questions.question: Quiet (default) output for `./hsim run rt_c_hello --mode csr`. Three lines from e2e.sh ([1/3], [2/3], [3/3]) are clean and progress-y. Do you want exactly those, or one final PASS/FAIL line only?

options.label: Pass through the three [N/3] lines (Recommended)
options.description: Stream stdout from tests/e2e.sh as-is in quiet mode. User sees QEMU1 / vsim / PASS as three short lines. --verbose adds stderr (QEMU/vsim chatter). ROADMAP says 'quiet 3-line progress output by default'.
options.preview:
[rt_c_hello 1/3 mode=csr] QEMU 1: drain entry state
[rt_c_hello 2/3 mode=csr] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2
[rt_c_hello 3/3 mode=csr] PASS: round-trip exit code 0

options.label: Single final line
options.description: Hide e2e.sh stdout, print only `hsim: rt_c_hello PASS (csr, 6.2s)` on success / `hsim: rt_c_hello FAIL (csr) -- log: /tmp/...` on failure. Tighter but loses the phase progress signal during long vsim phase.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# RED test for F3: `./hsim run <elf-path> --mode csr|pc`.
#
# Path-only contract (no bare-fixture-name shortcut). The positional
# argument is always an ELF path -- relative resolves against CWD,
# absolute is honored as-is. Wraps tests/e2e.sh with FIXTURE,
# TRIGGER_MODE, VSIM_ELF env vars.

set -uo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
HSIM="$ROOT/hsim"

fail() { echo "[FAIL] $*" >&2; exit 1; }
ok()   { echo "[ok] $*"; }

[ -x "$HSIM" ] || fail "missing executable: $HSIM"

cd "$ROOT"

# 1. --help mentions run + --mode + --verbose surface.
out=$("$HSIM" run --help 2>&1) || fail "run --help exit nonzero: $out"
echo "$out" | grep -q -- "--mode"    || fail "run --help missing --mode:\n$out"
echo "$out" | grep -q -- "--verbose" || fail "run --help missing --verbose:\n$out"
ok "run --help lists --mode and --verbose"

# 2. No positional -> argparse error, rc != 0.
set +e
out=$("$HSIM" run 2>&1); rc=$?
set -e
[ "$rc" -ne 0 ] || fail "run (no args) should exit non-zero, got 0; output: $out"
ok "run (no args) exits non-zero (rc=$rc)"

# 3. Missing --mode -> rc=2 with mode hint.
set +e
out=$("$HSIM" run tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf 2>&1); rc=$?
set -e
[ "$rc" -eq 2 ] || fail "run without --mode should rc=2, got $rc; output: $out"
echo "$out" | grep -qi "mode" || fail "missing --mode should hint at --mode:\n$out"
ok "run without --mode exits 2 with mode hint"

# 4. ELF path does not exist -> rc=2 with the offending path quoted.
set +e
out=$("$HSIM" run /no/such/foo.elf --mode csr 2>&1); rc=$?
set -e
[ "$rc" -eq 2 ] || fail "run nonexistent ELF should rc=2, got $rc; output: $out"
echo "$out" | grep -q "/no/such/foo.elf" || fail "missing ELF hint should mention the path:\n$out"
ok "run nonexistent ELF exits 2 with path hint"

# 5. vsim binary missing -> rc=2 with `vsim not built -- run ./hsim build`.
set +e
out=$(VSIM_BIN=/no/such/vsim "$HSIM" run tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf --mode csr 2>&1); rc=$?
set -e
[ "$rc" -eq 2 ] || fail "run with missing VSIM_BIN should rc=2, got $rc; output: $out"
echo "$out" | grep -qi "vsim not built" || fail "missing vsim hint required:\n$out"
echo "$out" | grep -q -- "./hsim build" || fail "hint should mention './hsim build':\n$out"
ok "run with VSIM_BIN missing exits 2 with build hint"

# 6. Smoke: real csr round-trip on rt_c_hello (artifacts assumed built).
#    ROADMAP requires <30s; cap higher to absorb host load variance.
SMOKE_TIMEOUT=60
set +e
out=$(timeout "$SMOKE_TIMEOUT" "$HSIM" run tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf --mode csr 2>&1); rc=$?
set -e
[ "$rc" -eq 0 ] || fail "smoke run failed rc=$rc; output:\n$out"
echo "$out" | grep -q "rt_c_hello 3/3" || fail "smoke output missing PASS line:\n$out"
ok "smoke run rt_c_hello --mode csr passes"

echo "PASS test_hsim_run_smoke"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""hsim - end-user entry point for the Andes hybrid simulator.

Single-file driver. Wraps the developer-grade scripts (setup.sh,
tests/e2e.sh, scripts/run_e2e.sh) behind four subcommands:

    ./hsim build [--what TARGET]
    ./hsim run <elf-or-fixture> --mode csr|pc
    ./hsim test [--filter REGEX] [--mode csr|pc|both]
    ./hsim list <fixtures|demos|modes>

Python 3 stdlib only; no pip deps.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import re
import shlex
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent


# --- F1: list ---------------------------------------------------------------

# M1..M8 short table. Authoritative source: docs/USER_GUIDE.md sections 3-4.
# Kept here so `./hsim list modes` does not need to grep USER_GUIDE at runtime.
MODES = [
    ("M1", "csr",           "csrwi 0x7C0, {0,1} insn match (default)"),
    ("M2", "pc",            "ELF symbols _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc"),
    ("M3", "icount",        "retired-insn count trigger"),
    ("M4", "bbv",           "basic-block vector profiling"),
    ("M5", "slice",         "slice-consume; vsim resumes from a captured slice"),
    ("M6", "archive",       "checkpoint archive replay"),
    ("M7", "replay",        "deterministic re-execution of a captured run"),
    ("M8", "end-of-program", "natural program exit terminates the round-trip"),
]


def parse_fixtures(makefile: Path) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
    """Extract fixture names + family from tests/fixtures/Makefile.

    Returns rows (name, family) in stable, family-grouped order. Family is
    the variable they came from (RT_FIXTURES, RT_C_FIXTURES, ...).
    """
    text = makefile.read_text()
    rows: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
    # Variable definitions look like:
    #   RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases \
    #                  rt_long_roi
    # We capture everything up to a non-continuation newline.
    var_pattern = re.compile(
        r"^(?P<var>[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*)\s*:?=\s*(?P<body>(?:[^\n]*\\\n)*[^\n]*)$",
        re.MULTILINE,
    )
    interesting = {
        "RT_FIXTURES":      "asm",
        "RT_C_FIXTURES":    "c",
        "RT_C_V_FIXTURES":  "c+rvv",
        "PROBE_V_FIXTURES": "probe",
    }
    seen: set[str] = set()
    for m in var_pattern.finditer(text):
        var = m.group("var")
        if var not in interesting:
            continue
        family = interesting[var]
        body = m.group("body").replace("\\\n", " ")
        for name in body.split():
            if name and name not in seen:
                rows.append((name, family))
                seen.add(name)
    # Pick up the legacy hand-listed ELF targets.
    for name in ("handoff_roundtrip",):
        if name not in seen:
            rows.append((name, "asm"))
            seen.add(name)
    return rows


def list_fixtures() -> int:
    makefile = REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "fixtures" / "Makefile"
    if not makefile.is_file():
        print(f"hsim: missing {makefile}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    rows = parse_fixtures(makefile)
    width = max(len(name) for name, _ in rows)
    print(f"{'FIXTURE'.ljust(width)}  FAMILY")
    for name, family in rows:
        print(f"{name.ljust(width)}  {family}")
    return 0


def list_demos() -> int:
    demo_dir = REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "demos"
    if not demo_dir.is_dir():
        print(f"hsim: missing {demo_dir}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    names = sorted(p.stem for p in demo_dir.glob("demo_*.sh"))
    if not names:
        print(f"hsim: no demos found under {demo_dir}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    for name in names:
        print(name)
    return 0


def list_modes() -> int:
    width_id = max(len(m[0]) for m in MODES)
    width_key = max(len(m[1]) for m in MODES)
    print(f"{'ID'.ljust(width_id)}  {'KEY'.ljust(width_key)}  DESCRIPTION")
    for mode_id, key, desc in MODES:
        print(f"{mode_id.ljust(width_id)}  {key.ljust(width_key)}  {desc}")
    return 0


def cmd_list(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    return {
        "fixtures": list_fixtures,
        "demos":    list_demos,
        "modes":    list_modes,
    }[args.target]()


# --- F2: build --------------------------------------------------------------

# Each target maps to a list of subprocess argv lists. We delegate to the
# existing scripts (setup.sh, scripts/build_*.sh, Makefiles) -- no logic
# duplicated here.
def _build_steps(target: str) -> list[list[str]]:
    qemu     = ["bash", "scripts/build_qemu.sh"]
    vsim     = ["bash", "scripts/build_vsim.sh"]
    plugin   = ["make", "-C", "qemu_plugin", "QEMU_ROOT=./QEMU"]
    simpoint = ["bash", "scripts/build_simpoint.sh"]
    fixtures = ["make", "-C", "tests/fixtures"]
    setup    = ["bash", "setup.sh"]
    return {
        "all":      [setup, fixtures],
        "qemu":     [qemu],
        "vsim":     [vsim],
        "plugin":   [plugin],
        "simpoint": [simpoint],
        "fixtures": [fixtures],
    }[target]


def cmd_build(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    try:
        steps = _build_steps(args.what)
    except KeyError:
        print(f"hsim: unknown build target: {args.what}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    for argv in steps:
        line = shlex.join(argv)
        if args.dry_run:
            print(line)
            continue
        print(f"[hsim] {line}")
        rc = subprocess.run(argv, cwd=REPO_ROOT).returncode
        if rc != 0:
            print(f"hsim: build step failed (rc={rc}): {line}", file=sys.stderr)
            return rc
    return 0


# --- main -------------------------------------------------------------------

def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="hsim",
        description="End-user driver for the Andes hybrid simulator (QEMU + vsim).",
    )
    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="cmd", metavar="<cmd>")
    sub.required = True

    p_list = sub.add_parser(
        "list",

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""hsim - end-user entry point for the Andes hybrid simulator.

Single-file driver. Wraps the developer-grade scripts (setup.sh,
tests/e2e.sh, scripts/run_e2e.sh) behind four subcommands:

    ./hsim build [--what TARGET]
    ./hsim run <elf-path> --mode csr|pc [--verbose]
    ./hsim test [--filter REGEX] [--mode csr|pc|both]
    ./hsim list <fixtures|demos|modes>

Python 3 stdlib only; no pip deps.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import os
import re
import shlex
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent


# --- F1: list ---------------------------------------------------------------

# M1..M8 short table. Authoritative source: docs/USER_GUIDE.md sections 3-4.
# Kept here so `./hsim list modes` does not need to grep USER_GUIDE at runtime.
MODES = [
    ("M1", "csr",           "csrwi 0x7C0, {0,1} insn match (default)"),
    ("M2", "pc",            "ELF symbols _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc"),
    ("M3", "icount",        "retired-insn count trigger"),
    ("M4", "bbv",           "basic-block vector profiling"),
    ("M5", "slice",         "slice-consume; vsim resumes from a captured slice"),
    ("M6", "archive",       "checkpoint archive replay"),
    ("M7", "replay",        "deterministic re-execution of a captured run"),
    ("M8", "end-of-program", "natural program exit terminates the round-trip"),
]


def parse_fixtures(makefile: Path) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
    """Extract fixture names + family from tests/fixtures/Makefile.

    Returns rows (name, family) in stable, family-grouped order. Family is
    the variable they came from (RT_FIXTURES, RT_C_FIXTURES, ...).
    """
    text = makefile.read_text()
    rows: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
    # Variable definitions look like:
    #   RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases \
    #                  rt_long_roi
    # We capture everything up to a non-continuation newline.
    var_pattern = re.compile(
        r"^(?P<var>[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*)\s*:?=\s*(?P<body>(?:[^\n]*\\\n)*[^\n]*)$",
        re.MULTILINE,
    )
    interesting = {
        "RT_FIXTURES":      "asm",
        "RT_C_FIXTURES":    "c",
        "RT_C_V_FIXTURES":  "c+rvv",
        "PROBE_V_FIXTURES": "probe",
    }
    seen: set[str] = set()
    for m in var_pattern.finditer(text):
        var = m.group("var")
        if var not in interesting:
            continue
        family = interesting[var]
        body = m.group("body").replace("\\\n", " ")
        for name in body.split():
            if name and name not in seen:
                rows.append((name, family))
                seen.add(name)
    # Pick up the legacy hand-listed ELF targets.
    for name in ("handoff_roundtrip",):
        if name not in seen:
            rows.append((name, "asm"))
            seen.add(name)
    return rows


def list_fixtures() -> int:
    makefile = REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "fixtures" / "Makefile"
    if not makefile.is_file():
        print(f"hsim: missing {makefile}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    rows = parse_fixtures(makefile)
    width = max(len(name) for name, _ in rows)
    print(f"{'FIXTURE'.ljust(width)}  FAMILY")
    for name, family in rows:
        print(f"{name.ljust(width)}  {family}")
    return 0


def list_demos() -> int:
    demo_dir = REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "demos"
    if not demo_dir.is_dir():
        print(f"hsim: missing {demo_dir}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    names = sorted(p.stem for p in demo_dir.glob("demo_*.sh"))
    if not names:
        print(f"hsim: no demos found under {demo_dir}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    for name in names:
        print(name)
    return 0


def list_modes() -> int:
    width_id = max(len(m[0]) for m in MODES)
    width_key = max(len(m[1]) for m in MODES)
    print(f"{'ID'.ljust(width_id)}  {'KEY'.ljust(width_key)}  DESCRIPTION")
    for mode_id, key, desc in MODES:
        print(f"{mode_id.ljust(width_id)}  {key.ljust(width_key)}  {desc}")
    return 0


def cmd_list(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    return {
        "fixtures": list_fixtures,
        "demos":    list_demos,
        "modes":    list_modes,
    }[args.target]()


# --- F2: build --------------------------------------------------------------

# Each target maps to a list of subprocess argv lists. We delegate to the
# existing scripts (setup.sh, scripts/build_*.sh, Makefiles) -- no logic
# duplicated here.
def _build_steps(target: str) -> list[list[str]]:
    qemu     = ["bash", "scripts/build_qemu.sh"]
    vsim     = ["bash", "scripts/build_vsim.sh"]
    plugin   = ["make", "-C", "qemu_plugin", "QEMU_ROOT=./QEMU"]
    simpoint = ["bash", "scripts/build_simpoint.sh"]
    fixtures = ["make", "-C", "tests/fixtures"]
    setup    = ["bash", "setup.sh"]
    return {
        "all":      [setup, fixtures],
        "qemu":     [qemu],
        "vsim":     [vsim],
        "plugin":   [plugin],
        "simpoint": [simpoint],
        "fixtures": [fixtures],
    }[target]


def cmd_build(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    try:
        steps = _build_steps(args.what)
    except KeyError:
        print(f"hsim: unknown build target: {args.what}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    for argv in steps:
        line = shlex.join(argv)
        if args.dry_run:
            print(line)
            continue
        print(f"[hsim] {line}")
        rc = subprocess.run(argv, cwd=REPO_ROOT).returncode
        if rc != 0:
            print(f"hsim: build step failed (rc={rc}): {line}", file=sys.stderr)
            return rc
    return 0


# --- main -------------------------------------------------------------------

def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="hsim",
        description="End-user driver for the Andes hybrid simulator (QEMU + vsim).",
    )
    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="cmd", metavar="<cmd>")
    sub.required = True

    p_list = sub.add_parser(
        "list",
        help="list available fixtures, demos, or trigger modes",
    )
    p_list.add_argument(
        "target",
        choices=("fixtures", "demos", "modes"),
        help="what to list",
    )
    p_list.set_defaults(func=cmd_list)

    p_build = sub.add_parser(
        "build",
        help="build artifacts (qemu, vsim, plugin, simpoint, ELF fixtures)",
    )
    p_build.add_argument(
        "--what",
        choices=("all", "qemu", "vsim", "plugin", "simpoint", "fixtures"),
        default="all",
        help="which target to build (default: all)",
    )
    p_build.add_argument(
        "--dry-run",
        action="store_true",
        help="print the underlying commands without executing",
    )
    p_build.set_defaults(func=cmd_build)

    return parser


def main(argv: list[str]) -> int:
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
    return args.func(args)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main(sys.argv[1:]))

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""hsim - end-user entry point for the Andes hybrid simulator.

Single-file driver. Wraps the developer-grade scripts (setup.sh,
tests/e2e.sh, scripts/run_e2e.sh) behind four subcommands:

    ./hsim build [--what TARGET]
    ./hsim run <elf-path> --mode csr|pc [--verbose]
    ./hsim test [--filter REGEX] [--mode csr|pc|both]
    ./hsim list <fixtures|demos|modes>

Python 3 stdlib only; no pip deps.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import os
import re
import shlex
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent


# --- F1: list ---------------------------------------------------------------

# M1..M8 short table. Authoritative source: docs/USER_GUIDE.md sections 3-4.
# Kept here so `./hsim list modes` does not need to grep USER_GUIDE at runtime.
MODES = [
    ("M1", "csr",           "csrwi 0x7C0, {0,1} insn match (default)"),
    ("M2", "pc",            "ELF symbols _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc"),
    ("M3", "icount",        "retired-insn count trigger"),
    ("M4", "bbv",           "basic-block vector profiling"),
    ("M5", "slice",         "slice-consume; vsim resumes from a captured slice"),
    ("M6", "archive",       "checkpoint archive replay"),
    ("M7", "replay",        "deterministic re-execution of a captured run"),
    ("M8", "end-of-program", "natural program exit terminates the round-trip"),
]


def parse_fixtures(makefile: Path) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
    """Extract fixture names + family from tests/fixtures/Makefile.

    Returns rows (name, family) in stable, family-grouped order. Family is
    the variable they came from (RT_FIXTURES, RT_C_FIXTURES, ...).
    """
    text = makefile.read_text()
    rows: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
    # Variable definitions look like:
    #   RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases \
    #                  rt_long_roi
    # We capture everything up to a non-continuation newline.
    var_pattern = re.compile(
        r"^(?P<var>[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*)\s*:?=\s*(?P<body>(?:[^\n]*\\\n)*[^\n]*)$",
        re.MULTILINE,
    )
    interesting = {
        "RT_FIXTURES":      "asm",
        "RT_C_FIXTURES":    "c",
        "RT_C_V_FIXTURES":  "c+rvv",
        "PROBE_V_FIXTURES": "probe",
    }
    seen: set[str] = set()
    for m in var_pattern.finditer(text):
        var = m.group("var")
        if var not in interesting:
            continue
        family = interesting[var]
        body = m.group("body").replace("\\\n", " ")
        for name in body.split():
            if name and name not in seen:
                rows.append((name, family))
                seen.add(name)
    # Pick up the legacy hand-listed ELF targets.
    for name in ("handoff_roundtrip",):
        if name not in seen:
            rows.append((name, "asm"))
            seen.add(name)
    return rows


def list_fixtures() -> int:
    makefile = REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "fixtures" / "Makefile"
    if not makefile.is_file():
        print(f"hsim: missing {makefile}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    rows = parse_fixtures(makefile)
    width = max(len(name) for name, _ in rows)
    print(f"{'FIXTURE'.ljust(width)}  FAMILY")
    for name, family in rows:
        print(f"{name.ljust(width)}  {family}")
    return 0


def list_demos() -> int:
    demo_dir = REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "demos"
    if not demo_dir.is_dir():
        print(f"hsim: missing {demo_dir}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    names = sorted(p.stem for p in demo_dir.glob("demo_*.sh"))
    if not names:
        print(f"hsim: no demos found under {demo_dir}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    for name in names:
        print(name)
    return 0


def list_modes() -> int:
    width_id = max(len(m[0]) for m in MODES)
    width_key = max(len(m[1]) for m in MODES)
    print(f"{'ID'.ljust(width_id)}  {'KEY'.ljust(width_key)}  DESCRIPTION")
    for mode_id, key, desc in MODES:
        print(f"{mode_id.ljust(width_id)}  {key.ljust(width_key)}  {desc}")
    return 0


def cmd_list(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    return {
        "fixtures": list_fixtures,
        "demos":    list_demos,
        "modes":    list_modes,
    }[args.target]()


# --- F2: build --------------------------------------------------------------

# Each target maps to a list of subprocess argv lists. We delegate to the
# existing scripts (setup.sh, scripts/build_*.sh, Makefiles) -- no logic
# duplicated here.
def _build_steps(target: str) -> list[list[str]]:
    qemu     = ["bash", "scripts/build_qemu.sh"]
    vsim     = ["bash", "scripts/build_vsim.sh"]
    plugin   = ["make", "-C", "qemu_plugin", "QEMU_ROOT=./QEMU"]
    simpoint = ["bash", "scripts/build_simpoint.sh"]
    fixtures = ["make", "-C", "tests/fixtures"]
    setup    = ["bash", "setup.sh"]
    return {
        "all":      [setup, fixtures],
        "qemu":     [qemu],
        "vsim":     [vsim],
        "plugin":   [plugin],
        "simpoint": [simpoint],
        "fixtures": [fixtures],
    }[target]


def cmd_build(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    try:
        steps = _build_steps(args.what)
    except KeyError:
        print(f"hsim: unknown build target: {args.what}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    for argv in steps:
        line = shlex.join(argv)
        if args.dry_run:
            print(line)
            continue
        print(f"[hsim] {line}")
        rc = subprocess.run(argv, cwd=REPO_ROOT).returncode
        if rc != 0:
            print(f"hsim: build step failed (rc={rc}): {line}", file=sys.stderr)
            return rc
    return 0


# --- F3: run ----------------------------------------------------------------

# Default build-artifact locations. Honored as env-var overrides so the
# F3 RED test can simulate a missing vsim binary without disturbing the
# real one.
_DEFAULT_ARTIFACTS = {
    "vsim":   REPO_ROOT / "verilator" / "build" / "sim_ax45mpv_premium",
    "qemu":   REPO_ROOT / "build" / "qemu" / "qemu-system-riscv64",
    "plugin": REPO_ROOT / "qemu_plugin" / "libhybrid_handoff.so",
}
_ARTIFACT_ENV = {"vsim": "VSIM_BIN", "qemu": "VSIM_QEMU", "plugin": "VSIM_PLUGIN"}


def _resolve_artifacts() -> dict[str, Path]:
    return {
        label: Path(os.environ.get(_ARTIFACT_ENV[label]) or default)
        for label, default in _DEFAULT_ARTIFACTS.items()
    }


def cmd_run(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    elf = Path(args.elf)
    if not elf.is_absolute():
        elf = (Path.cwd() / elf).resolve()
    if not elf.is_file():
        print(f"hsim: ELF not found: {args.elf}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2

    artifacts = _resolve_artifacts()
    for label, path in artifacts.items():
        if not path.is_file():
            print(f"hsim: {label} not built -- run ./hsim build", file=sys.stderr)
            return 2

    env = os.environ.copy()
    env["FIXTURE"]      = elf.stem
    env["TRIGGER_MODE"] = args.mode
    env["VSIM_ELF"]     = str(elf)
    env["VSIM_BIN"]     = str(artifacts["vsim"])
    env["VSIM_QEMU"]    = str(artifacts["qemu"])
    env["VSIM_PLUGIN"]  = str(artifacts["plugin"])

    cmd = ["bash", str(REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "e2e.sh")]
    if args.verbose:
        print(f"[hsim] FIXTURE={env['FIXTURE']} TRIGGER_MODE={env['TRIGGER_MODE']} "
              f"VSIM_ELF={env['VSIM_ELF']}")
        print(f"[hsim] exec: {shlex.join(cmd)}")
    return subprocess.run(cmd, cwd=REPO_ROOT, env=env).returncode


# --- main -------------------------------------------------------------------

def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="hsim",
        description="End-user driver for the Andes hybrid simulator (QEMU + vsim).",
    )
    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="cmd", metavar="<cmd>")
    sub.required = True

    p_list = sub.add_parser(
        "list",
        help="list available fixtures, demos, or trigger modes",
    )
    p_list.add_argument(
        "target",
        choices=("fixtures", "demos", "modes"),
        help="what to list",
    )
    p_list.set_defaults(func=cmd_list)

    p_build = sub.add_parser(
        "build",
        help="build artifacts (qemu, vsim, plugin, simpoint, ELF fixtures)",
    )
    p_build.add_argument(
        "--what",
        choices=("all", "qemu", "vsim", "plugin", "simpoint", "fixtures"),
        default="all",
        help="which target to build (default: all)",
    )
    p_build.add_argument(
        "--dry-run",
        action="store_true",
        help="print the underlying commands without executing",
    )
    p_build.set_defaults(func=cmd_build)

    return parser


def main(argv: list[str]) -> int:
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
    return args.func(args)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main(sys.argv[1:]))
