file.content:
# hybrid_sim

Workspace for the Andes hybrid simulator: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz) + vsim
(SystemC + Verilator, cycle-accurate) sharing memory and architectural state
across a magic-CSR boundary.

Two submodules:

- `QEMU/` - QEMU fork hosting the TCG plugin
- `verilator/` - vsim sources, including `hybrid/qemu_plugin/` (plugin source)

## Quickstart

```sh
git clone --recursive https://gitea.andestech.com/nick/hybrid_sim
cd hybrid_sim
./build.sh           # qemu, vsim, plugin (in that order)
./build.sh test      # ctest -L hybrid-e2e
```

Subcommands: `qemu`, `vsim`, `plugin`, `test`, `clean`. Run `./build.sh -h`
for the full list.

## Host dependencies

- C/C++ toolchain (clang preferred), CMake >= 3.20, ninja
- Python 3
- glib-2.0 dev (for the QEMU plugin)
- pkg-config
- Verilator (auto-detected; set `VSIM_VERILATOR_EXECUTABLE` if needed)
- libraries QEMU itself needs (pixman, slirp, etc. - see `QEMU/README.rst`)

## Outputs

After `./build.sh`:

- `QEMU/build/qemu-system-riscv64`
- `verilator/build/vsim`
- `verilator/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`

content.content:
1	# Hybrid Simulator User Guide
2	
3	Audience: a new engineer who wants to RUN the Andes hybrid simulator,
4	not study its internals. After this guide you can:
5	
6	1. Build every artifact from a fresh clone.
7	2. Configure QEMU, vsim, and the orchestrator for a workload.
8	3. Pick a switching mode (M1..M8) and run one round-trip end-to-end
9	   with a concrete worked example.
10	
11	For internals, read `docs/HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md`. For per-mode drill-down,
12	read `docs/switching_modes_tutorial.md`.
13	
14	---
15	
16	## 0. The one-paragraph mental model
17	
18	QEMU runs the boring parts at MHz. vsim (Verilator + SystemC) runs the
19	small region-of-interest at cycle accuracy. They share RAM via an
20	`mmap` file. A magic trigger (CSR write, PC, or instruction count)
21	hands control between them. One run is three legs:
22	
23	```
24	   QEMU1  -- state.bin + shared mmap -->  vsim  -- same files -->  QEMU2
25	   (fast)                                 (cycle accurate)         (fast)
26	```
27	
28	The thing you choose per run is the TRIGGER that decides where each
29	leg drains and the next leg resumes. That choice is the "switching
30	mode".
31	
32	---
33	
34	## 1. Build and environment setup
35	
36	### 1.1 Host prerequisites
37	
38	Install these on the host once:
39	
40	| Package          | Notes                                                |
41	|------------------|------------------------------------------------------|
42	| `git`            | clone the repo and its submodules                    |
43	| C/C++ toolchain  | clang or gcc; needed by QEMU and the plugin          |
44	| `cmake` >= 3.20  | vsim build                                           |
45	| `ninja`          | vsim default generator                               |
46	| `python3`        | orchestrator, plugin tests, demo scripts             |
47	| `glib-2.0` dev   | QEMU plugin link line                                |
48	| `pkg-config`     | QEMU configure                                       |
49	| `podman`         | runs vsim builds inside `vsim-env:latest` container  |
50	| Verilator        | auto-detected; set `VSIM_VERILATOR_EXECUTABLE` if needed |
51	| QEMU's own deps  | pixman, slirp, ... see `QEMU/README.rst`      |
52	| RISC-V toolchain | `riscv64-unknown-elf-{gcc,nm,objdump}` (Andes v5d)   |
53	
54	The Andes RISC-V toolchain is needed to build the fixtures and to
55	resolve symbols for PC-mode. The harness scripts default to
56	`/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/.../bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm`;
57	override with `NM=/your/path/to/nm` if you keep it elsewhere.
58	
59	### 1.2 Clone with submodules
60	
61	```sh
62	git clone --recursive https://gitea.andestech.com/nick/hybrid_sim
63	cd hybrid_sim
64	```
65	
66	If you forgot `--recursive`:
67	
68	```sh
69	git submodule update --init --recursive
70	```
71	
72	Submodules under this repo:
73	
74	| Path             | What it is                                          |
75	|------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|
76	| `QEMU/`   | QEMU 9.x fork that hosts the TCG plugin (branch: `hybrid`) |
77	| `verilator/`   | vsim sources (CMake + SystemC + Verilator)          |
78	| `tools/simpoint/`| SimPoint 3.2 clustering binary (UCSD)               |
79	
80	### 1.3 Per-host config (`config.env`)
81	
82	The repo ships a template `config.env.example` listing per-host paths
83	that cannot live in the build system (notably the Andes RISC-V
84	toolchain root, which `setup.sh` does NOT build).
85	
86	On the first `source ./setup.sh`, the template is copied to
87	`./config.env` (gitignored) so your edits never touch git history. If
88	the toolchain lives somewhere other than the default, edit
89	`config.env`:
90	
91	```sh
92	# config.env
93	HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN=/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
94	```
95	
96	The single variable today is:
97	
98	| Variable          | What it points at                                                  |
99	|-------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|
100	| `HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN`| Toolchain root. `$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/` must contain `riscv64-unknown-elf-{gcc,ld,nm,objdump,...}`. |
101	
102	How it propagates:
103	
104	- `setup.sh` sources `config.env`, validates `$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/`
105	  exists, and `export`s the variable so all child processes see it.
106	- The fixture `Makefile` (`tests/fixtures/Makefile`) defaults
107	  `TOOLCHAIN` to `$(HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN)` and aborts with a clear error if
108	  the variable is missing.
109	- The unified `e2e.sh` test harness loads `config.env` itself when run
110	  without prior `source ./setup.sh`, then derives
111	  `NM=$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm`.
112	
113	Override per-invocation without editing the file:
114	
115	```sh
116	HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN=/opt/my-toolchain make -C tests/fixtures
117	HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN=/opt/my-toolchain bash tests/demos/demo_pc_oneshot.sh
118	```
119	
120	### 1.4 Build everything
121	
122	One command builds all four artifacts and exports the env vars the
123	test harness expects:
124	
125	```sh
126	source ./setup.sh
127	```
128	
129	`source` (not `bash`) is required so the script can prepend the build
130	dirs to PATH and export `VSIM_QEMU`, `VSIM_PLUGIN`, `VSIM_BIN`,
131	`SIMPOINT_BIN`, `HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN` into the current shell. The steps it
132	runs:
133	
134	```
135	0/4 config     -> auto-copy config.env.example -> config.env (first run);
136	                  source it; export HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN
137	1/4 QEMU       -> build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64
138	2/4 vsim       -> verilator/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium    (in container)
139	3/4 plugin     -> qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so
140	4/4 SimPoint   -> tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint
141	```
142	
143	To rebuild just one component:
144	
145	```sh
146	./scripts/build_qemu.sh
147	./scripts/build_vsim.sh        # uses podman + Dockerfile (target `env`)
148	make -C qemu_plugin QEMU_ROOT=./QEMU
149	./scripts/build_simpoint.sh
150	```
151	
152	To clean:
153	
154	```sh
155	source ./setup.sh clean         # all four
156	source ./setup.sh clean qemu    # just QEMU build/
157	source ./setup.sh clean vsim
158	source ./setup.sh clean plugin
159	source ./setup.sh clean simpoint
160	```
161	
162	### 1.5 Build the test fixtures (RISC-V ELFs)
163	
164	Fixtures are small RISC-V programs that exercise specific transport
165	paths. They are NOT built by `setup.sh`; build them once:
166	
167	```sh
168	make -C tests/fixtures -j
169	```
170	
171	One fixture at a time:
172	
173	```sh
174	make -C tests/fixtures rt_c_v_regs.elf
175	```
176	
177	Output ELFs land next to the sources under
178	`tests/fixtures/*.elf`. The fixtures depend on the Andes
179	RISC-V GCC + the `libgloss_vh` semihosting library. The toolchain path
180	comes from `$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN` (see section 1.3); to override for one
181	invocation:
182	
183	```sh
184	make -C tests/fixtures TOOLCHAIN=/opt/my-toolchain
185	```
186	
187	### 1.6 Sanity check the build
188	
189	```sh
190	# All four artifacts present?
191	ls build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64
192	ls verilator/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium
193	ls qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so
194	ls tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint
195	
196	# Smallest possible end-to-end - one CSR-mode round-trip:
197	FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs TRIGGER_MODE=csr EXPECT_RC=0 \
198	  bash tests/e2e.sh
199	```
200	
201	Expected last line:
202	
203	```
204	[rt_c_v_regs 3/3 mode=csr] PASS: round-trip exit code 0
205	```
206	
207	If you see `skip: ... not built`, redo `source ./setup.sh` and
208	re-build fixtures (`make -C tests/fixtures`).
209	
210	---
211	
212	## 2. Configuring the simulator
213	
214	There are four configuration surfaces. Most users only touch the env
215	vars in 2.1.
216	
217	### 2.1 Environment variables (the only ones you usually set)
218	
219	`setup.sh` exports these for you. Override only if your artifacts live
220	somewhere non-default.
221	
222	| Variable        | Default                                          | Used by                  |
223	|-----------------|--------------------------------------------------|--------------------------|
224	| `HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN` | From `config.env` (sec 1.3); toolchain root  | Makefile, NM derivation  |
225	| `VSIM_QEMU`     | `build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64`                 | every harness            |
226	| `VSIM_PLUGIN`   | `qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`               | every harness            |
227	| `VSIM_BIN`      | first `verilator/build/sim_*`                  | every harness            |
228	| `VSIM_ELF`      | `tests/fixtures/$FIXTURE.elf`           | harness scripts          |
229	| `SIMPOINT_BIN`  | `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`                    | M4/T7 demos              |
230	| `NM`            | `$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm`   | PC-mode symbol resolve   |
231	| `TIMEOUT`       | 60 s (some demos use 120)                        | per-leg `timeout` cap    |
232	| `FIXTURE`       | `rt_shared_mem`                                  | which `.elf` to load     |
233	| `EXPECT_RC`     | 0                                                | expected QEMU2 exit code |
234	| `TRIGGER_MODE`  | `csr`                                            | `csr` or `pc`            |
235	| `JOBS`          | auto-cap on this 128-core host: set to 8         | parallel test runner     |
236	
237	Example: override the QEMU binary for one run:
238	
239	```sh
240	VSIM_QEMU=/opt/qemu-9.0/qemu-system-riscv64 \
241	  bash tests/demos/demo_csr_oneshot.sh
242	```
243	
244	### 2.2 The vsim CPU configuration YAML
245	
246	`verilator/config.yaml` selects which Andes core to verilate. The
247	default is `ax45mpv_premium`:
248	
249	```yaml
250	cpus:
251	  ax45mpv_premium:
252	    cpu_series: 45
253	    rtl_package: ax45mpv_premium
254	    cpp_wrapper: ax45mpv_premium_cpu_cluster_subsystem
255	    config: data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg
256	    config_dir: andes_ip/kv_core/top/hdl
257	    config_inc_filter: grep '^`define NDS_'
258	    extra_verilator_flags: --threads 8 --unroll-count 256
259	```
260	
261	Switch CPU by pointing CMake at a different YAML:
262	
263	```sh
264	cd verilator
265	cmake -S . -B build -G Ninja -DVSIM_CONFIG_FILE=my_other_cpu.yaml
266	cmake --build build -j
267	```
268	
269	`-DVSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON` registers the e2e fixtures with CTest;
270	`-DVSIM_NO_VERILATOR=ON` skips Verilator (use a prebuilt model).
271	
272	### 2.3 QEMU plugin args
273	
274	The plugin reads `arg=value` tokens after `-plugin <path>,`. They
275	select WHICH switching mode QEMU1 runs in:
276	
277	| Plugin arg           | Effect                                                                 |
278	|----------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------|
279	| `outfile=PATH`       | REQUIRED. Drained `hybrid_state_v1` is written here.                   |
280	| `enter_pc=0xADDR`    | M2: drain when PC == ADDR. Bypasses the CSR-insn matcher.              |
281	| `icount=N`           | M3: drain on the N-th retired insn (per vcpu).                         |
282	| `bbv_out=PATH`       | M4: emit one SimPoint BBV row per `slice=N` insns into PATH.           |
283	| `slice=N`            | Insns per slice (used by M4 and M5). Must be > 0.                      |
284	| `slice_at=K`         | M5: drain at the end of the K-th slice. Requires `slice=N`.            |
285	
286	If no mode arg is given, the default is CSR-insn match (M1): the
287	plugin matches `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` at retire.
288	
289	Example invocation (CSR mode, just the minimal arg):
290	
291	```
292	-plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=/tmp/state.bin
293	```
294	
295	Example (icount mode):
296	
297	```
298	-plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=/tmp/state.bin,icount=10000
299	```
300	
301	### 2.4 vsim CLI flags
302	
303	`sim_ax45mpv_premium <elf> [flags]`. The hybrid-relevant subset:
304	
305	| Flag                          | Purpose                                                  |
306	|-------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
307	| `--resume-from-qemu FILE`     | Read drained state, restore into hart, run from PC.     |
308	| `--handoff-out FILE`          | Drain back into FILE when the exit trigger fires.       |
309	| `--shared-mem-path FILE`      | mmap FILE as RAM (zero-copy share with QEMU).           |
310	| `--shared-mem-size BYTES`     | Size of the shared region. Match QEMU `-m`.             |
311	| `--shared-mem-base HEX`       | Guest physical address where file offset 0 lands.       |
312	| `--qemu-binary PATH`          | After drain, spawn this QEMU as Phase 3 (QEMU2).        |
313	| `--hybrid-exit-pc 0xADDR`     | M2 exit: drain when this PC retires.                    |
314	| `--hybrid-icount N`           | M3 exit: drain after N committed insns (vsim side).     |
315	| `--jtag-port N`               | Open the JTAG bitbang port (e.g. OpenOCD).              |
316	| `--log-level N`               | 0=trace .. 5=critical .. 6=off (default 2=info)         |
317	
318	Defaults for `--shared-mem-base` is `0x80000000` (the QEMU `virt` /
319	`andes_ae350` RAM base). Most users never override it.
320	
321	Run `$VSIM_BIN --help` for the full list (waveform/save/restore are
322	license-gated and off by default).
323	
324	### 2.5 Orchestrator plan YAML (used by M6/M7)
325	
326	For checkpoint workflows you describe a multi-step plan in YAML and
327	hand it to the orchestrator:
328	
329	```yaml
330	session:
331	  mem_size: 134217728            # 128 MB shared mmap
332	  mmap_path: null                # null -> mktemp in /dev/shm
333	  elf: tests/fixtures/rt_c_v_regs.elf
334	
335	steps:
336	  - model: qemu
337	    trigger: { kind: csr }       # M1 trigger; can also be { kind: pc, enter: 0x... } or { kind: icount, n: 5000 }
338	    on_exit: { archive_as: "snap/0" }
339	```
340	
341	Run it:
342	
343	```sh
344	python3 -m orchestrator.main run plan.yaml \
345	  --project-root . --workdir /tmp/run --archive-root /tmp/archives
346	```
347	
348	Replay:
349	
350	```sh
351	python3 -m orchestrator.main replay-checkpoint /tmp/archives/snap/0 \
352	  --resume-with vsim --project-root . --workdir /tmp/replay
353	```
354	
355	---
356	
357	## 3. Switching modes - which trigger to use
358	
359	| ID  | Trigger                                | Where        | When to pick it                                                |
360	|-----|----------------------------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|
361	| M1  | `csrwi 0x7C0, 0/1` insn match          | QEMU1 + vsim | Default. Your binary can embed `csrwi` markers.                |
362	| M2  | PC equals `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc`    | QEMU1 + vsim | Binary is yours, but you cannot insert CSR markers.            |
363	| M3  | Retired-instruction count              | QEMU1 + vsim | Binary is NOT yours (no CSR/symbol). Use a spin loop / blob.   |
364	| M4  | BBV emit + offline SimPoint cluster    | QEMU1 only   | Phase identification before the real run.                      |
365	| M5  | Drain at end of slice K                | QEMU1        | Replay one cluster representative slice on vsim.               |
366	| M6  | Archive state.bin + mem + meta after drain | orchestrator | Capture a portable, shippable checkpoint.                     |
367	| M7  | Replay a snap/K archive                | orchestrator | Resume the same checkpoint hours / days / hosts later.         |
368	| M8  | QEMU2 exits naturally via semihosting  | QEMU2        | Always. End-of-program signal.                                 |
369	
370	M1/M2/M3 are the three ways to delimit the ROI. M4..M7 stack on top
371	of them for SimPoint-style phase replay. M8 is automatic.
372	
373	Decision flow:
374	
375	```
376	Can you edit the binary?
377	  yes -> insert csrwi 0x7C0, 0/1 around the ROI -> M1
378	  no  -> Does it export _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc?
379	           yes -> M2
380	           no  -> Use icount  -> M3
381	You want phase-aware replay?  -> M4 + M5 + M6 + M7   (the T7 demo automates this)
382	```
383	
384	---
385	
386	## 4. Walk-through: one round-trip in each mode
387	
388	Each section is copy-pastable. They all share the same setup.
389	
390	### 4.0 Common setup (do once per shell)
391	
392	```sh
393	cd /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim
394	source ./setup.sh                         # builds + exports env vars
395	make -C tests/fixtures -j        # builds .elf fixtures
396	```
397	
398	Quick env check:
399	
400	```sh
401	echo "$VSIM_QEMU"
402	echo "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
403	echo "$VSIM_BIN"
404	ls tests/fixtures/rt_c_v_regs.elf
405	```
406	
407	### 4.1 M1 - CSR insn match (the default)
408	
409	Goal: run a vector fixture through QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 using the
410	two `csrwi 0x7C0, *` markers that the fixture already contains.
411	
412	```sh
413	bash tests/demos/demo_csr_oneshot.sh
414	```
415	
416	Expected last line:
417	
418	```
419	[demo M1] PASS (CSR insn match (M1+M8))
420	```
421	
422	The demo is a thin wrapper around the generic harness:
423	
424	```sh
425	FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs TRIGGER_MODE=csr EXPECT_RC=0 \
426	  bash tests/e2e.sh
427	```
428	
429	Inside, the three legs are:
430	
431	```
432	1) QEMU1 with -plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=$STATE
433	        -> drain at csrwi 0x7C0,0   -> exit 200
434	2) sim_ax45mpv_premium $ELF \
435	        --resume-from-qemu $STATE --handoff-out $STATE_V \
436	        --shared-mem-path $SHM --shared-mem-size 128MB \
437	        --qemu-binary $VSIM_QEMU
438	        -> ROI runs in vsim, drains at csrwi 0x7C0,1, spawns QEMU2
439	3) QEMU2 (spawned by vsim) restores state via gdbstub, continues,
440	        exits 0 via semihosting (M8).
441	```
442	
443	Try a different fixture:
444	
445	```sh
446	FIXTURE=rt_c_matmul TRIGGER_MODE=csr EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120 \
447	  bash tests/e2e.sh
448	```
449	
450	### 4.2 M2 - PC symbol resolve
451	
452	Same workload, but the trigger is a PC value from the symbol table.
453	
454	```sh
455	bash tests/demos/demo_pc_oneshot.sh
456	```
457	
458	To see what the demo passes under the hood:
459	
460	```sh
461	NM=/path/to/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm   # default in $NM if you ran setup.sh
462	
463	ENTER_PC=$("$NM" --defined-only tests/fixtures/rt_c_v_regs.elf \
464	            | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
465	EXIT_PC=$("$NM" --defined-only tests/fixtures/rt_c_v_regs.elf \
466	            | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
467	
468	echo "enter=$ENTER_PC  exit=$EXIT_PC"
469	
470	# QEMU1 leg
471	"$VSIM_QEMU" \
472	  -object memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=/dev/shm/share,size=134217728 \
473	  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
474	  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
475	  -nographic -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
476	  -bios tests/fixtures/rt_c_v_regs.elf \
477	  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=/tmp/q1.bin,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
478	
479	# vsim leg
480	"$VSIM_BIN" tests/fixtures/rt_c_v_regs.elf \
481	  --resume-from-qemu /tmp/q1.bin \
482	  --handoff-out /tmp/v.bin \
483	  --shared-mem-path /dev/shm/share \
484	  --shared-mem-size 134217728 \
485	  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
486	  --hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC"
487	```
488	
489	The fixture binary is unchanged from M1; only the orchestrator
490	arguments are different.
491	
492	### 4.3 M3 - icount round-trip (no markers)
493	
494	For workloads you cannot edit. Both legs drain on a retired-insn count
495	target.
496	
497	```sh
498	bash tests/demos/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh
499	```
500	
501	Override the targets:
502	
503	```sh
504	QEMU_ICOUNT=20000 VSIM_ICOUNT=8000 \
505	  bash tests/demos/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh
506	```
507	
508	What you can verify by hand: the demo extracts the drained PC from
509	`state.bin` at byte offset 16, little-endian u64. For `icount_spin.elf`
510	the loop is two RVC insns at `0x100002` and `0x100004`:
511	
512	```sh
513	python3 -c "
514	import struct
515	print(hex(struct.unpack('<Q', open('/tmp/icount_state.bin','rb').read()[16:24])[0]))
516	"
517	```
518	
519	### 4.4 M4 - SimPoint BBV profile pass
520	
521	QEMU1 emits one BBV row per slice; the offline `simpoint` tool
522	clusters them. No vsim leg.
523	
524	```sh
525	WORKDIR=/tmp/m4-profile bash tests/demos/demo_bbv_profile.sh
526	```
527	
528	Plugin args under the hood:
529	
530	```
531	-plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=$STATE,bbv_out=$WORKDIR/profile.bb,slice=1000,icount=10000
532	```
533	
534	That writes 10 BBV rows (`icount / slice`). Then:
535	
536	```
537	simpoint -loadFVFile $WORKDIR/profile.bb -maxK 2 -dim 2 \
538	         -saveSimpoints $WORKDIR/simpts \
539	         -saveSimpointWeights $WORKDIR/weights
540	```
541	
542	`simpts` lines: `<slice_idx> <cluster_id>`. `weights` lines:
543	`<mass> <cluster_id>`. Tune `SLICE`, `ICOUNT`, `MAXK` via env vars.
544	
545	### 4.5 M5 - slice-consume replay entry
546	
547	Given a `K` chosen from M4's `simpts` output, drain QEMU at the END of
548	slice K so vsim resumes from exactly that point.
549	
550	```sh
551	WORKDIR=/tmp/m4-profile bash tests/demos/demo_bbv_profile.sh
552	SIMPTS=/tmp/m4-profile/simpts \
553	  bash tests/demos/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh
554	```
555	
556	Manual K:
557	
558	```sh
559	SLICE=1000 SLICE_AT=3 bash tests/demos/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh
560	```
561	
562	Plugin args:
563	
564	```
565	-plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=$STATE,slice=1000,slice_at=3,icount=10000
566	```
567	
568	`icount=` is a safety fallback if K is invalid.
569	
570	### 4.6 M6 + M7 - archive checkpoint and replay
571	
572	Capture a portable archive `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` from a
573	single QEMU drain, then resume it on vsim (or QEMU) in a separate
574	process.
575	
576	```sh
577	WORKDIR=/tmp/m6m7 bash tests/demos/demo_archive_replay.sh
578	```
579	
580	Replay on QEMU instead of vsim:
581	
582	```sh
583	RESUME_WITH=qemu WORKDIR=/tmp/m6m7-qemu \
584	  bash tests/demos/demo_archive_replay.sh
585	```
586	
587	The orchestrator commands you can run by hand:
588	
589	```sh
590	python3 -m orchestrator.main run plan.yaml \
591	  --project-root . \
592	  --workdir /tmp/m6m7/run \
593	  --archive-root /tmp/m6m7
594	# -> /tmp/m6m7/snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}
595	
596	python3 -m orchestrator.main replay-checkpoint /tmp/m6m7/snap/0 \
597	  --resume-with vsim \
598	  --project-root . \
599	  --workdir /tmp/m6m7/replay
600	```
601	
602	### 4.7 T7 - the centerpiece (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)
603	
604	One command chains all four phases on `rt_c_v_regs.elf`:
605	
606	```sh
607	WORKDIR=/tmp/t7 bash tests/demos/demo_simpoint_loop.sh
608	```
609	
610	Expected last line:
611	
612	```
613	[demo T7] PASS (Full SimPoint loop (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)): bbv_rows=15 phases=2 replays_ok=2 workdir=/tmp/t7
614	```
615	
616	To run a different fixture, set `VSIM_ELF` and tune `SLICE` so the
617	BBV pass yields 10..30 rows:
618	
619	```sh
620	VSIM_ELF=tests/fixtures/rt_c_matmul.elf \
621	  SLICE=50 PROFILE_ICOUNT=4000 MAXK=3 \
622	  WORKDIR=/tmp/t7-matmul \
623	  bash tests/demos/demo_simpoint_loop.sh
624	```
625	
626	---
627	
628	## 5. Running the full test suite
629	
630	After any change to the simulator or plugin, run the suite:
631	
632	```sh
633	JOBS=8 bash scripts/run_e2e.sh
634	```
635	
636	`JOBS=8` is the practical cap on this 128-core host (vsim Verilator
637	processes are CPU-hungry; the default cap of 128 saturates the box and
638	times out). Logs go to `/tmp/e2e-<RAND>/`; failed-test logs are
639	streamed at the end.
640	
641	Run a subset:
642	
643	```sh
644	FILTER=demo  JOBS=8 bash scripts/run_e2e.sh         # only the M1..M8 demos
645	FILTER=rt_c_v JOBS=8 bash scripts/run_e2e.sh        # only V-related fixtures
646	MODE=csr     JOBS=8 bash scripts/run_e2e.sh         # skip the pc-mode iterations
647	JOBS=1                     bash scripts/run_e2e.sh  # sequential for debugging
648	LOG_DIR=/tmp/my-logs       bash scripts/run_e2e.sh
649	```
650	
651	Single-test sequential demo runner (one banner per test):
652	
653	```sh
654	cd tests/fixtures
655	make demo
656	# or
657	FILTER='csr|pc' bash demo.sh
658	```
659	
660	CTest entry per demo:
661	
662	```sh
663	ctest --test-dir verilator/build -L switching-mode --output-on-failure
664	ctest --test-dir verilator/build -R demo_csr      # one demo
665	```
666	
667	---
668	
669	## 6. Common failures and how to read them
670	
671	| Symptom                                                | Cause / fix                                                          |
672	|--------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|
673	| `skip: ... not built; run 'make' ...`                  | `source ./setup.sh` and `make -C tests/fixtures`            |
674	| `QEMU 1 expected exit 200, got 0`                      | Trigger never fired. Wrong mode / wrong PC / wrong icount value.     |
675	| `QEMU 1 expected exit 200, got 124`                    | `timeout` killed the plugin. Increase `TIMEOUT` or tune the target.  |
676	| `missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols`     | M2 needs a fixture with those labels. Rebuild or use M1.             |
677	| `vsim drained pc=0x... not in icount_spin loop`        | `VSIM_ICOUNT` too small / wrong fixture for M3.                      |
678	| Whole suite times out under `scripts/run_e2e.sh`               | Set `JOBS=8`. Default 128 saturates a 128-core host.                 |
679	| `[FAIL] [N/M] ... -> /tmp/e2e-XXXX/...log`             | Open that log; it is the full stdout+stderr of the failing leg.      |
680	
681	---
682	
683	## 7. Where to go next
684	
685	- `docs/HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md` - architecture deep-dive (state ABI,
686	  Debug Module transport, V-scratch trick, passthrough rationale).
687	- `docs/switching_modes_tutorial.md` - one exercise per M1..M8 with
688	  drill-down on the underlying CLI invocations.
689	- `CLAUDE.md` - file-level pointers (key files, build commands,
690	  fixture authoring rules).
691	- `tests/fixtures/rt_c_v_regs.c` - reference fixture
692	  combining V regfile + V scalar CSRs + shared memory.
693

file.content:
# Hybrid Simulator User Guide

Audience: a new engineer who wants to RUN the Andes hybrid simulator,
not study its internals. After this guide you can:

1. Build every artifact from a fresh clone.
2. Configure QEMU, vsim, and the orchestrator for a workload.
3. Pick a switching mode (M1..M8) and run one round-trip end-to-end
   with a concrete worked example.

For internals, read `docs/HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md`. For per-mode drill-down,
read `docs/switching_modes_tutorial.md`.

---

## 0. The one-paragraph mental model

QEMU runs the boring parts at MHz. vsim (Verilator + SystemC) runs the
small region-of-interest at cycle accuracy. They share RAM via an
`mmap` file. A magic trigger (CSR write, PC, or instruction count)
hands control between them. One run is three legs:

```
   QEMU1  -- state.bin + shared mmap -->  vsim  -- same files -->  QEMU2
   (fast)                                 (cycle accurate)         (fast)
```

The thing you choose per run is the TRIGGER that decides where each
leg drains and the next leg resumes. That choice is the "switching
mode".

---

## 1. Build and environment setup

### 1.1 Host prerequisites

Install these on the host once:

| Package          | Notes                                                |
|------------------|------------------------------------------------------|
| `git`            | clone the repo and its submodules                    |
| C/C++ toolchain  | clang or gcc; needed by QEMU and the plugin          |
| `cmake` >= 3.20  | vsim build                                           |
| `ninja`          | vsim default generator                               |
| `python3`        | orchestrator, plugin tests, demo scripts             |
| `glib-2.0` dev   | QEMU plugin link line                                |
| `pkg-config`     | QEMU configure                                       |
| `podman`         | runs vsim builds inside `vsim-env:latest` container  |
| Verilator        | auto-detected; set `VSIM_VERILATOR_EXECUTABLE` if needed |
| QEMU's own deps  | pixman, slirp, ... see `QEMU/README.rst`      |
| RISC-V toolchain | `riscv64-unknown-elf-{gcc,nm,objdump}` (Andes v5d)   |

The Andes RISC-V toolchain is needed to build the fixtures and to
resolve symbols for PC-mode. The harness scripts default to
`/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/.../bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm`;
override with `NM=/your/path/to/nm` if you keep it elsewhere.

### 1.2 Clone with submodules

```sh
git clone --recursive https://gitea.andestech.com/nick/hybrid_sim
cd hybrid_sim
```

If you forgot `--recursive`:

```sh
git submodule update --init --recursive
```

Submodules under this repo:

| Path             | What it is                                          |
|------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| `QEMU/`   | QEMU 9.x fork that hosts the TCG plugin (branch: `hybrid`) |
| `verilator/`   | vsim sources (CMake + SystemC + Verilator)          |
| `tools/simpoint/`| SimPoint 3.2 clustering binary (UCSD)               |

### 1.3 Per-host config (`config.env`)

The repo ships a template `config.env.example` listing per-host paths
that cannot live in the build system (notably the Andes RISC-V
toolchain root, which `setup.sh` does NOT build).

On the first `source ./setup.sh`, the template is copied to
`./config.env` (gitignored) so your edits never touch git history. If
the toolchain lives somewhere other than the default, edit
`config.env`:

```sh
# config.env
HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN=/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
```

The single variable today is:

| Variable          | What it points at                                                  |
|-------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN`| Toolchain root. `$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/` must contain `riscv64-unknown-elf-{gcc,ld,nm,objdump,...}`. |

How it propagates:

- `setup.sh` sources `config.env`, validates `$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/`
  exists, and `export`s the variable so all child processes see it.
- The fixture `Makefile` (`tests/fixtures/Makefile`) defaults
  `TOOLCHAIN` to `$(HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN)` and aborts with a clear error if
  the variable is missing.
- The unified `e2e.sh` test harness loads `config.env` itself when run
  without prior `source ./setup.sh`, then derives
  `NM=$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm`.

Override per-invocation without editing the file:

```sh
HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN=/opt/my-toolchain make -C tests/fixtures
HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN=/opt/my-toolchain bash tests/demos/demo_pc_oneshot.sh
```

### 1.4 Build everything

One command builds all four artifacts and exports the env vars the
test harness expects:

```sh
source ./setup.sh
```

`source` (not `bash`) is required so the script can prepend the build
dirs to PATH and export `VSIM_QEMU`, `VSIM_PLUGIN`, `VSIM_BIN`,
`SIMPOINT_BIN`, `HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN` into the current shell. The steps it
runs:

```
0/4 config     -> auto-copy config.env.example -> config.env (first run);
                  source it; export HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN
1/4 QEMU       -> build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64
2/4 vsim       -> verilator/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium    (in container)
3/4 plugin     -> qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so
4/4 SimPoint   -> tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint
```

To rebuild just one component:

```sh
./scripts/build_qemu.sh
./scripts/build_vsim.sh        # uses podman + Dockerfile (target `env`)
make -C qemu_plugin QEMU_ROOT=./QEMU
./scripts/build_simpoint.sh
```

To clean:

```sh
source ./setup.sh clean         # all four
source ./setup.sh clean qemu    # just QEMU build/
source ./setup.sh clean vsim
source ./setup.sh clean plugin
source ./setup.sh clean simpoint
```

### 1.5 Build the test fixtures (RISC-V ELFs)

Fixtures are small RISC-V programs that exercise specific transport
paths. They are NOT built by `setup.sh`; build them once:

```sh
make -C tests/fixtures -j
```

One fixture at a time:

```sh
make -C tests/fixtures rt_c_v_regs.elf
```

Output ELFs land next to the sources under
`tests/fixtures/*.elf`. The fixtures depend on the Andes
RISC-V GCC + the `libgloss_vh` semihosting library. The toolchain path
comes from `$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN` (see section 1.3); to override for one
invocation:

```sh
make -C tests/fixtures TOOLCHAIN=/opt/my-toolchain
```

### 1.6 Sanity check the build

```sh
# All four artifacts present?
ls build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64
ls verilator/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium
ls qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so
ls tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint

# Smallest possible end-to-end - one CSR-mode round-trip:
FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs TRIGGER_MODE=csr EXPECT_RC=0 \
  bash tests/e2e.sh
```

Expected last line:

```
[rt_c_v_regs 3/3 mode=csr] PASS: round-trip exit code 0
```

If you see `skip: ... not built`, redo `source ./setup.sh` and
re-build fixtures (`make -C tests/fixtures`).

---

## 2. Configuring the simulator

There are four configuration surfaces. Most users only touch the env
vars in 2.1.

### 2.1 Environment variables (the only ones you usually set)

`setup.sh` exports these for you. Override only if your artifacts live
somewhere non-default.

| Variable        | Default                                          | Used by                  |
|-----------------|--------------------------------------------------|--------------------------|
| `HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN` | From `config.env` (sec 1.3); toolchain root  | Makefile, NM derivation  |
| `VSIM_QEMU`     | `build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64`                 | every harness            |
| `VSIM_PLUGIN`   | `qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`               | every harness            |
| `VSIM_BIN`      | first `verilator/build/sim_*`                  | every harness            |
| `VSIM_ELF`      | `tests/fixtures/$FIXTURE.elf`           | harness scripts          |
| `SIMPOINT_BIN`  | `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`                    | M4/T7 demos              |
| `NM`            | `$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm`   | PC-mode symbol resolve   |
| `TIMEOUT`       | 60 s (some demos use 120)                        | per-leg `timeout` cap    |
| `FIXTURE`       | `rt_shared_mem`                                  | which `.elf` to load     |
| `EXPECT_RC`     | 0                                                | expected QEMU2 exit code |
| `TRIGGER_MODE`  | `csr`                                            | `csr` or `pc`            |
| `JOBS`          | auto-cap on this 128-core host: set to 8         | parallel test runner     |

Example: override the QEMU binary for one run:

```sh
VSIM_QEMU=/opt/qemu-9.0/qemu-system-riscv64 \
  bash tests/demos/demo_csr_oneshot.sh
```

### 2.2 The vsim CPU configuration YAML

`verilator/config.yaml` selects which Andes core to verilate. The
default is `ax45mpv_premium`:

```yaml
cpus:
  ax45mpv_premium:
    cpu_series: 45
    rtl_package: ax45mpv_premium
    cpp_wrapper: ax45mpv_premium_cpu_cluster_subsystem
    config: data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg
    config_dir: andes_ip/kv_core/top/hdl
    config_inc_filter: grep '^`define NDS_'
    extra_verilator_flags: --threads 8 --unroll-count 256
```

Switch CPU by pointing CMake at a different YAML:

```sh
cd verilator
cmake -S . -B build -G Ninja -DVSIM_CONFIG_FILE=my_other_cpu.yaml
cmake --build build -j
```

`-DVSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON` registers the e2e fixtures with CTest;
`-DVSIM_NO_VERILATOR=ON` skips Verilator (use a prebuilt model).

### 2.3 QEMU plugin args

The plugin reads `arg=value` tokens after `-plugin <path>,`. They
select WHICH switching mode QEMU1 runs in:

| Plugin arg           | Effect                                                                 |
|----------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `outfile=PATH`       | REQUIRED. Drained `hybrid_state_v1` is written here.                   |
| `enter_pc=0xADDR`    | M2: drain when PC == ADDR. Bypasses the CSR-insn matcher.              |
| `icount=N`           | M3: drain on the N-th retired insn (per vcpu).                         |
| `bbv_out=PATH`       | M4: emit one SimPoint BBV row per `slice=N` insns into PATH.           |
| `slice=N`            | Insns per slice (used by M4 and M5). Must be > 0.                      |
| `slice_at=K`         | M5: drain at the end of the K-th slice. Requires `slice=N`.            |

If no mode arg is given, the default is CSR-insn match (M1): the
plugin matches `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` at retire.

Example invocation (CSR mode, just the minimal arg):

```
-plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=/tmp/state.bin
```

Example (icount mode):

```
-plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=/tmp/state.bin,icount=10000
```

### 2.4 vsim CLI flags

`sim_ax45mpv_premium <elf> [flags]`. The hybrid-relevant subset:

| Flag                          | Purpose                                                  |
|-------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
| `--resume-from-qemu FILE`     | Read drained state, restore into hart, run from PC.     |
| `--handoff-out FILE`          | Drain back into FILE when the exit trigger fires.       |
| `--shared-mem-path FILE`      | mmap FILE as RAM (zero-copy share with QEMU).           |
| `--shared-mem-size BYTES`     | Size of the shared region. Match QEMU `-m`.             |
| `--shared-mem-base HEX`       | Guest physical address where file offset 0 lands.       |
| `--qemu-binary PATH`          | After drain, spawn this QEMU as Phase 3 (QEMU2).        |
| `--hybrid-exit-pc 0xADDR`     | M2 exit: drain when this PC retires.                    |
| `--hybrid-icount N`           | M3 exit: drain after N committed insns (vsim side).     |
| `--jtag-port N`               | Open the JTAG bitbang port (e.g. OpenOCD).              |
| `--log-level N`               | 0=trace .. 5=critical .. 6=off (default 2=info)         |

Defaults for `--shared-mem-base` is `0x80000000` (the QEMU `virt` /
`andes_ae350` RAM base). Most users never override it.

Run `$VSIM_BIN --help` for the full list (waveform/save/restore are
license-gated and off by default).

### 2.5 Orchestrator plan YAML (used by M6/M7)

For checkpoint workflows you describe a multi-step plan in YAML and
hand it to the orchestrator:

```yaml
session:
  mem_size: 134217728            # 128 MB shared mmap
  mmap_path: null                # null -> mktemp in /dev/shm
  elf: tests/fixtures/rt_c_v_regs.elf

steps:
  - model: qemu
    trigger: { kind: csr }       # M1 trigger; can also be { kind: pc, enter: 0x... } or { kind: icount, n: 5000 }
    on_exit: { archive_as: "snap/0" }
```

Run it:

```sh
python3 -m orchestrator.main run plan.yaml \
  --project-root . --workdir /tmp/run --archive-root /tmp/archives
```

Replay:

```sh
python3 -m orchestrator.main replay-checkpoint /tmp/archives/snap/0 \
  --resume-with vsim --project-root . --workdir /tmp/replay
```

---

## 3. Switching modes - which trigger to use

| ID  | Trigger                                | Where        | When to pick it                                                |
|-----|----------------------------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|
| M1  | `csrwi 0x7C0, 0/1` insn match          | QEMU1 + vsim | Default. Your binary can embed `csrwi` markers.                |
| M2  | PC equals `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc`    | QEMU1 + vsim | Binary is yours, but you cannot insert CSR markers.            |
| M3  | Retired-instruction count              | QEMU1 + vsim | Binary is NOT yours (no CSR/symbol). Use a spin loop / blob.   |
| M4  | BBV emit + offline SimPoint cluster    | QEMU1 only   | Phase identification before the real run.                      |
| M5  | Drain at end of slice K                | QEMU1        | Replay one cluster representative slice on vsim.               |
| M6  | Archive state.bin + mem + meta after drain | orchestrator | Capture a portable, shippable checkpoint.                     |
| M7  | Replay a snap/K archive                | orchestrator | Resume the same checkpoint hours / days / hosts later.         |
| M8  | QEMU2 exits naturally via semihosting  | QEMU2        | Always. End-of-program signal.                                 |

M1/M2/M3 are the three ways to delimit the ROI. M4..M7 stack on top
of them for SimPoint-style phase replay. M8 is automatic.

Decision flow:

```
Can you edit the binary?
  yes -> insert csrwi 0x7C0, 0/1 around the ROI -> M1
  no  -> Does it export _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc?
           yes -> M2
           no  -> Use icount  -> M3
You want phase-aware replay?  -> M4 + M5 + M6 + M7   (the T7 demo automates this)
```

---

## 4. Walk-through: one round-trip in each mode

Each section is copy-pastable. They all share the same setup.

### 4.0 Common setup (do once per shell)

```sh
cd /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim
source ./setup.sh                         # builds + exports env vars
make -C tests/fixtures -j        # builds .elf fixtures
```

Quick env check:

```sh
echo "$VSIM_QEMU"
echo "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
echo "$VSIM_BIN"
ls tests/fixtures/rt_c_v_regs.elf
```

### 4.1 M1 - CSR insn match (the default)

Goal: run a vector fixture through QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 using the
two `csrwi 0x7C0, *` markers that the fixture already contains.

```sh
bash tests/demos/demo_csr_oneshot.sh
```

Expected last line:

```
[demo M1] PASS (CSR insn match (M1+M8))
```

The demo is a thin wrapper around the generic harness:

```sh
FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs TRIGGER_MODE=csr EXPECT_RC=0 \
  bash tests/e2e.sh
```

Inside, the three legs are:

```
1) QEMU1 with -plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=$STATE
        -> drain at csrwi 0x7C0,0   -> exit 200
2) sim_ax45mpv_premium $ELF \
        --resume-from-qemu $STATE --handoff-out $STATE_V \
        --shared-mem-path $SHM --shared-mem-size 128MB \
        --qemu-binary $VSIM_QEMU
        -> ROI runs in vsim, drains at csrwi 0x7C0,1, spawns QEMU2
3) QEMU2 (spawned by vsim) restores state via gdbstub, continues,
        exits 0 via semihosting (M8).
```

Try a different fixture:

```sh
FIXTURE=rt_c_matmul TRIGGER_MODE=csr EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120 \
  bash tests/e2e.sh
```

### 4.2 M2 - PC symbol resolve

Same workload, but the trigger is a PC value from the symbol table.

```sh
bash tests/demos/demo_pc_oneshot.sh
```

To see what the demo passes under the hood:

```sh
NM=/path/to/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm   # default in $NM if you ran setup.sh

ENTER_PC=$("$NM" --defined-only tests/fixtures/rt_c_v_regs.elf \
            | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
EXIT_PC=$("$NM" --defined-only tests/fixtures/rt_c_v_regs.elf \
            | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')

echo "enter=$ENTER_PC  exit=$EXIT_PC"

# QEMU1 leg
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=/dev/shm/share,size=134217728 \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios tests/fixtures/rt_c_v_regs.elf \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=/tmp/q1.bin,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"

# vsim leg
"$VSIM_BIN" tests/fixtures/rt_c_v_regs.elf \
  --resume-from-qemu /tmp/q1.bin \
  --handoff-out /tmp/v.bin \
  --shared-mem-path /dev/shm/share \
  --shared-mem-size 134217728 \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  --hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC"
```

The fixture binary is unchanged from M1; only the orchestrator
arguments are different.

### 4.3 M3 - icount round-trip (no markers)

For workloads you cannot edit. Both legs drain on a retired-insn count
target.

```sh
bash tests/demos/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh
```

Override the targets:

```sh
QEMU_ICOUNT=20000 VSIM_ICOUNT=8000 \
  bash tests/demos/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh
```

What you can verify by hand: the demo extracts the drained PC from
`state.bin` at byte offset 16, little-endian u64. For `icount_spin.elf`
the loop is two RVC insns at `0x100002` and `0x100004`:

```sh
python3 -c "
import struct
print(hex(struct.unpack('<Q', open('/tmp/icount_state.bin','rb').read()[16:24])[0]))
"
```

### 4.4 M4 - SimPoint BBV profile pass

QEMU1 emits one BBV row per slice; the offline `simpoint` tool
clusters them. No vsim leg.

```sh
WORKDIR=/tmp/m4-profile bash tests/demos/demo_bbv_profile.sh
```

Plugin args under the hood:

```
-plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=$STATE,bbv_out=$WORKDIR/profile.bb,slice=1000,icount=10000
```

That writes 10 BBV rows (`icount / slice`). Then:

```
simpoint -loadFVFile $WORKDIR/profile.bb -maxK 2 -dim 2 \
         -saveSimpoints $WORKDIR/simpts \
         -saveSimpointWeights $WORKDIR/weights
```

`simpts` lines: `<slice_idx> <cluster_id>`. `weights` lines:
`<mass> <cluster_id>`. Tune `SLICE`, `ICOUNT`, `MAXK` via env vars.

### 4.5 M5 - slice-consume replay entry

Given a `K` chosen from M4's `simpts` output, drain QEMU at the END of
slice K so vsim resumes from exactly that point.

```sh
WORKDIR=/tmp/m4-profile bash tests/demos/demo_bbv_profile.sh
SIMPTS=/tmp/m4-profile/simpts \
  bash tests/demos/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh
```

Manual K:

```sh
SLICE=1000 SLICE_AT=3 bash tests/demos/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh
```

Plugin args:

```
-plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=$STATE,slice=1000,slice_at=3,icount=10000
```

`icount=` is a safety fallback if K is invalid.

### 4.6 M6 + M7 - archive checkpoint and replay

Capture a portable archive `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` from a
single QEMU drain, then resume it on vsim (or QEMU) in a separate
process.

```sh
WORKDIR=/tmp/m6m7 bash tests/demos/demo_archive_replay.sh
```

Replay on QEMU instead of vsim:

```sh
RESUME_WITH=qemu WORKDIR=/tmp/m6m7-qemu \
  bash tests/demos/demo_archive_replay.sh
```

The orchestrator commands you can run by hand:

```sh
python3 -m orchestrator.main run plan.yaml \
  --project-root . \
  --workdir /tmp/m6m7/run \
  --archive-root /tmp/m6m7
# -> /tmp/m6m7/snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}

python3 -m orchestrator.main replay-checkpoint /tmp/m6m7/snap/0 \
  --resume-with vsim \
  --project-root . \
  --workdir /tmp/m6m7/replay
```

### 4.7 T7 - the centerpiece (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)

One command chains all four phases on `rt_c_v_regs.elf`:

```sh
WORKDIR=/tmp/t7 bash tests/demos/demo_simpoint_loop.sh
```

Expected last line:

```
[demo T7] PASS (Full SimPoint loop (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)): bbv_rows=15 phases=2 replays_ok=2 workdir=/tmp/t7
```

To run a different fixture, set `VSIM_ELF` and tune `SLICE` so the
BBV pass yields 10..30 rows:

```sh
VSIM_ELF=tests/fixtures/rt_c_matmul.elf \
  SLICE=50 PROFILE_ICOUNT=4000 MAXK=3 \
  WORKDIR=/tmp/t7-matmul \
  bash tests/demos/demo_simpoint_loop.sh
```

---

## 5. Running the full test suite

After any change to the simulator or plugin, run the suite:

```sh
JOBS=8 bash scripts/run_e2e.sh
```

`JOBS=8` is the practical cap on this 128-core host (vsim Verilator
processes are CPU-hungry; the default cap of 128 saturates the box and
times out). Logs go to `/tmp/e2e-<RAND>/`; failed-test logs are
streamed at the end.

Run a subset:

```sh
FILTER=demo  JOBS=8 bash scripts/run_e2e.sh         # only the M1..M8 demos
FILTER=rt_c_v JOBS=8 bash scripts/run_e2e.sh        # only V-related fixtures
MODE=csr     JOBS=8 bash scripts/run_e2e.sh         # skip the pc-mode iterations
JOBS=1                     bash scripts/run_e2e.sh  # sequential for debugging
LOG_DIR=/tmp/my-logs       bash scripts/run_e2e.sh
```

Single-test sequential demo runner (one banner per test):

```sh
cd tests/fixtures
make demo
# or
FILTER='csr|pc' bash demo.sh
```

CTest entry per demo:

```sh
ctest --test-dir verilator/build -L switching-mode --output-on-failure
ctest --test-dir verilator/build -R demo_csr      # one demo
```

---

## 6. Common failures and how to read them

| Symptom                                                | Cause / fix                                                          |
|--------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `skip: ... not built; run 'make' ...`                  | `source ./setup.sh` and `make -C tests/fixtures`            |
| `QEMU 1 expected exit 200, got 0`                      | Trigger never fired. Wrong mode / wrong PC / wrong icount value.     |
| `QEMU 1 expected exit 200, got 124`                    | `timeout` killed the plugin. Increase `TIMEOUT` or tune the target.  |
| `missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols`     | M2 needs a fixture with those labels. Rebuild or use M1.             |
| `vsim drained pc=0x... not in icount_spin loop`        | `VSIM_ICOUNT` too small / wrong fixture for M3.                      |
| Whole suite times out under `scripts/run_e2e.sh`               | Set `JOBS=8`. Default 128 saturates a 128-core host.                 |
| `[FAIL] [N/M] ... -> /tmp/e2e-XXXX/...log`             | Open that log; it is the full stdout+stderr of the failing leg.      |

---

## 7. Where to go next

- `docs/HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md` - architecture deep-dive (state ABI,
  Debug Module transport, V-scratch trick, passthrough rationale).
- `docs/switching_modes_tutorial.md` - one exercise per M1..M8 with
  drill-down on the underlying CLI invocations.
- `CLAUDE.md` - file-level pointers (key files, build commands,
  fixture authoring rules).
- `tests/fixtures/rt_c_v_regs.c` - reference fixture
  combining V regfile + V scalar CSRs + shared memory.

1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	* Always update docs/USER_GUIDE.md if something changed.
5	
6	## Repository Overview
7	
8	Andes hybrid RISC-V co-simulator: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz-level) + vsim (SystemC + Verilator, cycle-accurate) share architectural state across a magic-CSR boundary. The system runs a three-phase round-trip: **QEMU1** (drain on CSR/PC trigger) -> **vsim** (resume + execute + drain) -> **QEMU2** (restore via gdbstub, continue).
9	
10	Two Git submodules:
11	- `QEMU/` - QEMU fork hosting the TCG plugin (branch: `hybrid`)
12	- `verilator/` - vsim sources; `hybrid/` subdirs hold the plugin and test fixtures
13	- `tools/simpoint/` - SimPoint 3.2 (UCSD) clustering binary; pinned to upstream `17783e5`
14	
15	Shared ABI lives at `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` (not inside either submodule).
16	
17	## Build Commands
18	
19	```sh
20	# Build everything (qemu -> vsim -> plugin -> simpoint)
21	source ./setup.sh
22	
23	# Build individual components
24	./scripts/build_qemu.sh                              # QEMU (configure + make -j64)
25	./scripts/build_vsim.sh                              # vsim (Docker/podman, runs ./build.sh inside container)
26	make -C qemu_plugin QEMU_ROOT=./QEMU  # TCG plugin (out-of-tree)
27	./scripts/build_simpoint.sh                          # SimPoint clustering binary (g++ on host)
28	
29	# Build RISC-V ELF test fixtures
30	make -C tests/fixtures              # all fixtures
31	make -C tests/fixtures rt_c_v_regs.elf  # single fixture
32	
33	# Clean
34	source ./setup.sh clean [qemu|vsim|plugin|simpoint|all]
35	```
36	
37	**Outputs:**
38	- `build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64`
39	- `verilator/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium` (or other CPU variant)
40	- `qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`
41	- `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`
42	
43	`scripts/build_vsim.sh` is Docker-based (podman). It builds an image from `verilator/Dockerfile` (target `env`) then runs `./build.sh` inside it, mounting the repo at `/work` and `include/` as `/include:ro`.
44	
45	## Running Tests
46	
47	```sh
48	# All e2e tests in parallel (auto-detects job count from nproc + /dev/shm headroom)
49	bash scripts/run_e2e.sh
50	
51	# Run with specific options
52	JOBS=4 MODE=csr bash scripts/run_e2e.sh         # 4-way parallel, CSR-mode only
53	JOBS=1 bash scripts/run_e2e.sh                  # sequential (debug)
54	FILTER=rt_c_v bash scripts/run_e2e.sh           # regex filter on fixture name
55	LOG_DIR=/tmp/my-logs bash scripts/run_e2e.sh    # custom log directory
56	
57	# Unit tests (inside vsim build container)
58	# The container build runs: cmake --build build -j
59	# To run ctest directly after build:
60	#   podman run ... ./build.sh && podman exec ... ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-e2e
61	```
62	
63	**Important:** on a high-core-count machine (128+ cores) `default_jobs()` computes `min(nproc, /dev/shm_free/256MB)` which can reach 128+. Running that many vsim Verilator processes simultaneously saturates CPU and causes timeouts. Use `JOBS=8` (or lower) as a practical cap: `JOBS=8 bash scripts/run_e2e.sh`.
64	
65	`scripts/run_e2e.sh` exits 0 only when all tests pass. Logs go to a `mktemp` directory under `/tmp`; path is printed at the start of each run. Each test's full output lands in `$LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log`.
66	
67	## Architecture
68	
69	### Three-Phase Handoff
70	
71	```
72	QEMU1 --(state file)--> vsim --(state file + V-scratch mmap)--> QEMU2
73	```
74	
75	1. **QEMU plugin** (`qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c`) - TCG plugin. On CSR-write trigger (`csrwi 0x7C0, 0`) or PC-trigger, drains QEMU's architectural state into a binary `hybrid_state_v1` struct written to `--outfile`.
76	2. **vsim handoff controller** (`src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp`) - Detects the trigger inside vsim, pauses the hart via DMI, then calls `ResumeDriver` to restore state.
77	3. **ResumeDriver** (`src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`) - Writes GPRs/FPRs/CSRs/V-state into the vsim hart via RISC-V Debug Module Abstract Commands and Program Buffer.
78	4. **StateDrain** (`src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp`) - On vsim exit trigger, reads back the full architectural state via DMI Abstract Commands.
79	5. **qemu_handback** (`src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp`) - Spawns QEMU2, connects via gdbstub, sends P-packet register writes for GPRs/FPRs/CSRs/V-regs, then `continue`s.
80	
81	### Trigger Modes
82	
83	- **CSR mode** (default): Pattern-matches `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` (entry) / `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` (exit) at retire time. CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write on Andes ax45mp.
84	- **PC mode**: ELF symbols `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc` resolved at startup; CSR insns are bypassed.
85	
86	Both modes coexist in the fixtures. `TRIGGER_MODE=csr|pc` selects at runtime.
87	
88	### Shared ABI (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`)
89	
90	`hybrid_state_v1` is the binary wire format exchanged between QEMU plugin, vsim, and the handback path. It holds: 32 GPRs, 32 FPRs, M-mode CSRs (mstatus, mepc, etc.), PMP (64 pmpaddr + 16 pmpcfg), and the V extension block (vstart, vtype, vl, vlenb, 32 vreg[], vxsat, vxrm, vcsr). `HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX=64` supports VLEN up to 512 bits.
91	
92	Flags (`HYBRID_FLAG_RV64`, `HYBRID_FLAG_FP`, `HYBRID_FLAG_V`) gate which state blocks are live. The ABI struct is validated by `tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp`.
93	
94	### V-State Transport
95	
96	V register transport uses a fixed scratch region (`kVScratchGpa = 0x80FF0000`) in the shared mmap. vsim's inline asm writes all 32 V regs there via `vse8.v`; `StateDrain` reads those bytes into `hybrid_state_v1::vreg[]`. `qemu_handback` ships them to QEMU2 via gdbstub P-packets by register number.
97	
98	V scalar CSR transport (vtype, vl, vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr):
99	- **Tier A** (Abstract Commands): vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr, mstatus.VS
100	- **Tier B** (Program Buffer): vtype + vl via `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` (direct CSR write to vtype sets vill; vl is read-only; only `vsetvl` restores both atomically)
101	
102	Restore order in `ResumeDriver::resume()` when `HYBRID_FLAG_V` is set:
103	1. `mstatus.VS` (enables V in the hart)
104	2. `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` in Program Buffer (stages `vl` in x5, `vtype` in x6 via abstract GPR writes first)
105	3. `vstart` (AFTER vsetvl: vsetvl resets vstart to 0)
106	4. `vxsat`, `vxrm`, `vcsr`
107	5. GPR loop (overwrites the x5/x6 scratch - safe now)
108	
109	### Test Fixtures
110	
111	All fixtures run on the unified `tests/hybrid/e2e.sh` harness, which
112	sets up an `/dev/shm`-backed shared mmap (128 MB) so memory survives
113	the QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 round-trip. Two fixture families:
114	
115	| Family   | Examples                                                    | Notes |
116	|----------|-------------------------------------------------------------|-------|
117	| asm      | `handoff_roundtrip`, `rt_all_gprs`, `rt_csr_survival`, `rt_shared_mem` | no C runtime; don't strictly need shared mmap but use it anyway (~20 ms cost is negligible). |
118	| C-level  | `rt_c_hello`, `rt_c_matmul`, `rt_c_v_regs`, `rt_c_v_csrs`   | linked against vsim-demo C runtime. Shared mmap is REQUIRED -- the stack/heap and `main()`'s ra-spill/restore around `csrwi 0x7C0,1` must survive vsim -> QEMU2. |
119	
120	The shared mmap is created with `mktemp -p /dev/shm` + `truncate`,
121	then QEMU is launched with `-object memory-backend-file`.
122	
123	Fixture source lives in `tests/fixtures/`. C-level fixtures link `runtime/{crt0.S, trap.S, handler.c}` plus the Andes toolchain's `-lgloss_vh` (semihosting-backed libgloss), using `rt_c.ld`. V-using fixtures require `-march=rv64gcv`. **Phase-2 rule:** the span between `enter_vsim()` and `exit_vsim()` runs on vsim and MUST NOT issue any syscall (no `printf`, `fopen`, semihosting ebreak, etc.) -- vsim does not implement semihosting, so any libgloss-routed call inside the ROI traps. Phase 1 and phase 3 print via standard newlib stdio (both QEMUs are launched with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`).
124	
125	### vsim CMake Structure
126	
127	vsim lives entirely in `verilator/`. Key CMake options:
128	- `VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID` (default OFF) - enables e2e CTest registration
129	- `VSIM_CONFIG_FILE` - CPU YAML (default `config.yaml`)
130	- `VSIM_NO_VERILATOR` - skip Verilator build (assume prebuilt)
131	
132	The hybrid orchestration layer (`src/hybrid/*.hpp`) is header-only and depends only on C++20 standard library + the shared ABI header. SystemC/Verilator are not required for hybrid unit tests.
133	
134	Hybrid unit tests (`tests/hybrid/`) use [doctest](https://github.com/doctest/doctest). They are registered in `cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` and run via `ctest`. The `recording_bus.hpp` / `RecordingSession` patterns provide duck-typed test doubles for the DMI bus and gdbstub session without requiring virtual interfaces.
135	
136	## Key Files
137	
138	| File | Purpose |
139	|------|---------|
140	| `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` | Wire-format struct (shared by all three components) |
141	| `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` | TCG plugin: drain on trigger |
142	| `verilator/src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp` | Phase dispatcher |
143	| `verilator/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp` | DMI state restore (Tier A+B) |
144	| `verilator/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` | DMI state capture |
145	| `verilator/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` | Spawn QEMU2, gdbstub P-packets |
146	| `verilator/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp` | V register transport via scratch region |
147	| `verilator/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` | CTest registration for all e2e fixtures |
148	| `scripts/run_e2e.sh` | Parallel e2e runner (FIFO job pool, per-test logs) |
149	| `tests/fixtures/Makefile` | RISC-V ELF fixture build (Andes toolchain) |
150

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
* Always update docs/USER_GUIDE.md if something changed.

## Repository Overview

Andes hybrid RISC-V co-simulator: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz-level) + vsim (SystemC + Verilator, cycle-accurate) share architectural state across a magic-CSR boundary. The system runs a three-phase round-trip: **QEMU1** (drain on CSR/PC trigger) -> **vsim** (resume + execute + drain) -> **QEMU2** (restore via gdbstub, continue).

Two Git submodules:
- `QEMU/` - QEMU fork hosting the TCG plugin (branch: `hybrid`)
- `verilator/` - vsim sources; `hybrid/` subdirs hold the plugin and test fixtures
- `tools/simpoint/` - SimPoint 3.2 (UCSD) clustering binary; pinned to upstream `17783e5`

Shared ABI lives at `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` (not inside either submodule).

## Build Commands

```sh
# Build everything (qemu -> vsim -> plugin -> simpoint)
source ./setup.sh

# Build individual components
./scripts/build_qemu.sh                              # QEMU (configure + make -j64)
./scripts/build_vsim.sh                              # vsim (Docker/podman, runs ./build.sh inside container)
make -C qemu_plugin QEMU_ROOT=./QEMU  # TCG plugin (out-of-tree)
./scripts/build_simpoint.sh                          # SimPoint clustering binary (g++ on host)

# Build RISC-V ELF test fixtures
make -C tests/fixtures              # all fixtures
make -C tests/fixtures rt_c_v_regs.elf  # single fixture

# Clean
source ./setup.sh clean [qemu|vsim|plugin|simpoint|all]
```

**Outputs:**
- `build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64`
- `verilator/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium` (or other CPU variant)
- `qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`
- `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`

`scripts/build_vsim.sh` is Docker-based (podman). It builds an image from `verilator/Dockerfile` (target `env`) then runs `./build.sh` inside it, mounting the repo at `/work` and `include/` as `/include:ro`.

## Running Tests

```sh
# All e2e tests in parallel (auto-detects job count from nproc + /dev/shm headroom)
bash scripts/run_e2e.sh

# Run with specific options
JOBS=4 MODE=csr bash scripts/run_e2e.sh         # 4-way parallel, CSR-mode only
JOBS=1 bash scripts/run_e2e.sh                  # sequential (debug)
FILTER=rt_c_v bash scripts/run_e2e.sh           # regex filter on fixture name
LOG_DIR=/tmp/my-logs bash scripts/run_e2e.sh    # custom log directory

# Unit tests (inside vsim build container)
# The container build runs: cmake --build build -j
# To run ctest directly after build:
#   podman run ... ./build.sh && podman exec ... ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-e2e
```

**Important:** on a high-core-count machine (128+ cores) `default_jobs()` computes `min(nproc, /dev/shm_free/256MB)` which can reach 128+. Running that many vsim Verilator processes simultaneously saturates CPU and causes timeouts. Use `JOBS=8` (or lower) as a practical cap: `JOBS=8 bash scripts/run_e2e.sh`.

`scripts/run_e2e.sh` exits 0 only when all tests pass. Logs go to a `mktemp` directory under `/tmp`; path is printed at the start of each run. Each test's full output lands in `$LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log`.

## Architecture

### Three-Phase Handoff

```
QEMU1 --(state file)--> vsim --(state file + V-scratch mmap)--> QEMU2
```

1. **QEMU plugin** (`qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c`) - TCG plugin. On CSR-write trigger (`csrwi 0x7C0, 0`) or PC-trigger, drains QEMU's architectural state into a binary `hybrid_state_v1` struct written to `--outfile`.
2. **vsim handoff controller** (`src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp`) - Detects the trigger inside vsim, pauses the hart via DMI, then calls `ResumeDriver` to restore state.
3. **ResumeDriver** (`src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`) - Writes GPRs/FPRs/CSRs/V-state into the vsim hart via RISC-V Debug Module Abstract Commands and Program Buffer.
4. **StateDrain** (`src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp`) - On vsim exit trigger, reads back the full architectural state via DMI Abstract Commands.
5. **qemu_handback** (`src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp`) - Spawns QEMU2, connects via gdbstub, sends P-packet register writes for GPRs/FPRs/CSRs/V-regs, then `continue`s.

### Trigger Modes

- **CSR mode** (default): Pattern-matches `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` (entry) / `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` (exit) at retire time. CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write on Andes ax45mp.
- **PC mode**: ELF symbols `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc` resolved at startup; CSR insns are bypassed.

Both modes coexist in the fixtures. `TRIGGER_MODE=csr|pc` selects at runtime.

### Shared ABI (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`)

`hybrid_state_v1` is the binary wire format exchanged between QEMU plugin, vsim, and the handback path. It holds: 32 GPRs, 32 FPRs, M-mode CSRs (mstatus, mepc, etc.), PMP (64 pmpaddr + 16 pmpcfg), and the V extension block (vstart, vtype, vl, vlenb, 32 vreg[], vxsat, vxrm, vcsr). `HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX=64` supports VLEN up to 512 bits.

Flags (`HYBRID_FLAG_RV64`, `HYBRID_FLAG_FP`, `HYBRID_FLAG_V`) gate which state blocks are live. The ABI struct is validated by `tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp`.

### V-State Transport

V register transport uses a fixed scratch region (`kVScratchGpa = 0x80FF0000`) in the shared mmap. vsim's inline asm writes all 32 V regs there via `vse8.v`; `StateDrain` reads those bytes into `hybrid_state_v1::vreg[]`. `qemu_handback` ships them to QEMU2 via gdbstub P-packets by register number.

V scalar CSR transport (vtype, vl, vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr):
- **Tier A** (Abstract Commands): vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr, mstatus.VS
- **Tier B** (Program Buffer): vtype + vl via `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` (direct CSR write to vtype sets vill; vl is read-only; only `vsetvl` restores both atomically)

Restore order in `ResumeDriver::resume()` when `HYBRID_FLAG_V` is set:
1. `mstatus.VS` (enables V in the hart)
2. `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` in Program Buffer (stages `vl` in x5, `vtype` in x6 via abstract GPR writes first)
3. `vstart` (AFTER vsetvl: vsetvl resets vstart to 0)
4. `vxsat`, `vxrm`, `vcsr`
5. GPR loop (overwrites the x5/x6 scratch - safe now)

### Test Fixtures

All fixtures run on the unified `tests/hybrid/e2e.sh` harness, which
sets up an `/dev/shm`-backed shared mmap (128 MB) so memory survives
the QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 round-trip. Two fixture families:

| Family   | Examples                                                    | Notes |
|----------|-------------------------------------------------------------|-------|
| asm      | `handoff_roundtrip`, `rt_all_gprs`, `rt_csr_survival`, `rt_shared_mem` | no C runtime; don't strictly need shared mmap but use it anyway (~20 ms cost is negligible). |
| C-level  | `rt_c_hello`, `rt_c_matmul`, `rt_c_v_regs`, `rt_c_v_csrs`   | linked against vsim-demo C runtime. Shared mmap is REQUIRED -- the stack/heap and `main()`'s ra-spill/restore around `csrwi 0x7C0,1` must survive vsim -> QEMU2. |

The shared mmap is created with `mktemp -p /dev/shm` + `truncate`,
then QEMU is launched with `-object memory-backend-file`.

Fixture source lives in `tests/fixtures/`. C-level fixtures link `runtime/{crt0.S, trap.S, handler.c}` plus the Andes toolchain's `-lgloss_vh` (semihosting-backed libgloss), using `rt_c.ld`. V-using fixtures require `-march=rv64gcv`. **Phase-2 rule:** the span between `enter_vsim()` and `exit_vsim()` runs on vsim and MUST NOT issue any syscall (no `printf`, `fopen`, semihosting ebreak, etc.) -- vsim does not implement semihosting, so any libgloss-routed call inside the ROI traps. Phase 1 and phase 3 print via standard newlib stdio (both QEMUs are launched with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`).

### vsim CMake Structure

vsim lives entirely in `verilator/`. Key CMake options:
- `VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID` (default OFF) - enables e2e CTest registration
- `VSIM_CONFIG_FILE` - CPU YAML (default `config.yaml`)
- `VSIM_NO_VERILATOR` - skip Verilator build (assume prebuilt)

The hybrid orchestration layer (`src/hybrid/*.hpp`) is header-only and depends only on C++20 standard library + the shared ABI header. SystemC/Verilator are not required for hybrid unit tests.

Hybrid unit tests (`tests/hybrid/`) use [doctest](https://github.com/doctest/doctest). They are registered in `cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` and run via `ctest`. The `recording_bus.hpp` / `RecordingSession` patterns provide duck-typed test doubles for the DMI bus and gdbstub session without requiring virtual interfaces.

## Key Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` | Wire-format struct (shared by all three components) |
| `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` | TCG plugin: drain on trigger |
| `verilator/src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp` | Phase dispatcher |
| `verilator/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp` | DMI state restore (Tier A+B) |
| `verilator/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` | DMI state capture |
| `verilator/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` | Spawn QEMU2, gdbstub P-packets |
| `verilator/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp` | V register transport via scratch region |
| `verilator/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` | CTest registration for all e2e fixtures |
| `scripts/run_e2e.sh` | Parallel e2e runner (FIFO job pool, per-test logs) |
| `tests/fixtures/Makefile` | RISC-V ELF fixture build (Andes toolchain) |

# hybrid_sim

Workspace for the Andes hybrid simulator: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz) + vsim
(SystemC + Verilator, cycle-accurate) sharing memory and architectural state
across a magic-CSR boundary.

Two submodules:

- `QEMU/` - QEMU fork hosting the TCG plugin
- `verilator/` - vsim sources, including `hybrid/qemu_plugin/` (plugin source)

## Quickstart

```sh
git clone --recursive https://gitea.andestech.com/nick/hybrid_sim
cd hybrid_sim
./build.sh           # qemu, vsim, plugin (in that order)
./build.sh test      # ctest -L hybrid-e2e
```

Subcommands: `qemu`, `vsim`, `plugin`, `test`, `clean`. Run `./build.sh -h`
for the full list.

## Host dependencies

- C/C++ toolchain (clang preferred), CMake >= 3.20, ninja
- Python 3
- glib-2.0 dev (for the QEMU plugin)
- pkg-config
- Verilator (auto-detected; set `VSIM_VERILATOR_EXECUTABLE` if needed)
- libraries QEMU itself needs (pixman, slirp, etc. - see `QEMU/README.rst`)

## Outputs

After `./build.sh`:

- `QEMU/build/qemu-system-riscv64`
- `verilator/build/vsim`
- `verilator/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`

---

## 0.5 Quick start with `./hsim`

`./hsim` is the canonical end-user entry point. It wraps the
developer-grade scripts documented in Sections 1-5 (`setup.sh`,
`tests/e2e.sh`, `scripts/run_e2e.sh`) behind four subcommands.

Four-command worked example - cold checkout to passing suite:

```sh
./hsim build                                            # qemu, vsim, plugin, simpoint, fixtures
./hsim list fixtures                                    # discover available fixtures
./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf --mode csr     # one CSR-mode round-trip
./hsim test                                             # full e2e suite (defaults: MODE=both, JOBS=8)
```

Expected last line of `./hsim test`:

```
PASS 45, FAIL 0, SKIP 0 (elapsed: <Ns>)
```

Subcommand cheat sheet:

| Command                                                          | What it does                                                        |
|------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `./hsim list <fixtures|demos|modes>`                             | List available fixtures, demos, or trigger modes (M1..M8).          |
| `./hsim build [--what TARGET] [--dry-run]`                       | Build artifacts; targets: `all`, `qemu`, `vsim`, `plugin`, `simpoint`, `fixtures`. |
| `./hsim run <elf> --mode csr|pc [-v]`                            | Run one ELF through the QEMU -> vsim -> QEMU round-trip.            |
| `./hsim test [--filter REGEX] [--mode csr|pc|both] [--jobs N]`   | Run the e2e suite; defaults `--mode both --jobs 8`.                 |

Run `./hsim <cmd> -h` for the full flag list of a subcommand. The rest
of this guide (Sections 1-7) documents the underlying scripts and is
useful when you need to bypass `./hsim` (custom env vars, single-leg
debugging, alternative CPU YAML, etc.).

---

## 1. Build and environment setup
